Every non-exited warp lane must execute the same instruction and qualifiers. Integer overflow is wrapping; runtime validation is not executed on a GPU.

Its LLVM source record uses A, B, C, metadata, selector order. The selector must be the compile-time constant `0`. Every non-exited warp lane must execute the same instruction and qualifiers. Every 4-bit metadata group must be `0x4`, `0x8`, `0x9`, `0xc`, `0xd`, or `0xe`; any other value is undefined behavior. Integer overflow uses finite saturation. Runtime validation is not executed on a GPU.
- `mma_sp_ordered_metadata_m16n8k64_s32_u8_satfinite` takes fragments in C, A, B, metadata, selector order and lowers to one convergent, register-only `mma.sp::ordered_metadata.sync.aligned.m16n8k64.row.col.satfinite.s32.u8.u8.s32` instruction. Its LLVM source record uses A, B, C, metadata, selector order. The selector must be the compile-time constant `0`. Every non-exited warp lane must execute the same instruction and qualifiers. Every 4-bit metadata group must be `0x4`, `0x8`, `0x9`, `0xc`, `0xd`, or `0xe`; any other value is undefined behavior. Integer overflow uses finite saturation. Runtime validation is not executed on a GPU.

## Packed-atomic contracts

- `packed_atomic_add_bf16x2` (`bf16x2`): the native PTX instruction starts at `sm_90`; cuda-oxide admits it from sm_90+. Omitted `.sem` / `.scope` mean relaxed GPU scope. Each 16-bit element rounds to nearest-even and `.noftz` preserves subnormal inputs and results. The elements are atomic independently, so the returned `u32` contains old per-element values that may not form one coherent pair. The pointer must address four writable, four-byte-aligned global bytes; do not mix whole-word or non-atomic overlapping access, and every racing atomic must use a mutually inclusive scope.
- `packed_atomic_add_f16x2` (`f16x2`): the native PTX instruction starts at `sm_60`; cuda-oxide admits it from sm_70+. Omitted `.sem` / `.scope` mean relaxed GPU scope. Each 16-bit element rounds to nearest-even and `.noftz` preserves subnormal inputs and results. The elements are atomic independently, so the returned `u32` contains old per-element values that may not form one coherent pair. The pointer must address four writable, four-byte-aligned global bytes; do not mix whole-word or non-atomic overlapping access, and every racing atomic must use a mutually inclusive scope.

## Redux contracts

- `redux_sync_add`: raw and dialect operands are `[member_mask, value]`, adapted to LLVM `(value, membermask)`. The executing lane must be named in the mask, and every non-exited named lane must execute the same instruction with the same qualifiers and mask.
- `redux_sync_and`: raw and dialect operands are `[member_mask, value]`, adapted to LLVM `(value, membermask)`. The executing lane must be named in the mask, and every non-exited named lane must execute the same instruction with the same qualifiers and mask.
- `redux_sync_max_i32`: raw and dialect operands are `[member_mask, value]`, adapted to LLVM `(value, membermask)`. The executing lane must be named in the mask, and every non-exited named lane must execute the same instruction with the same qualifiers and mask.
- `redux_sync_max_u32`: raw and dialect operands are `[member_mask, value]`, adapted to LLVM `(value, membermask)`. The executing lane must be named in the mask, and every non-exited named lane must execute the same instruction with the same qualifiers and mask.
- `redux_sync_min_i32`: raw and dialect operands are `[member_mask, value]`, adapted to LLVM `(value, membermask)`. The executing lane must be named in the mask, and every non-exited named lane must execute the same instruction with the same qualifiers and mask.
- `redux_sync_min_u32`: raw and dialect operands are `[member_mask, value]`, adapted to LLVM `(value, membermask)`. The executing lane must be named in the mask, and every non-exited named lane must execute the same instruction with the same qualifiers and mask.
- `redux_sync_or`: raw and dialect operands are `[member_mask, value]`, adapted to LLVM `(value, membermask)`. The executing lane must be named in the mask, and every non-exited named lane must execute the same instruction with the same qualifiers and mask.
- `redux_sync_xor`: raw and dialect operands are `[member_mask, value]`, adapted to LLVM `(value, membermask)`. The executing lane must be named in the mask, and every non-exited named lane must execute the same instruction with the same qualifiers and mask.

## Packed-ALU contracts

- `abs_bf16x2` carries one packed `bf16x2` value in a `u32` and lowers to one pure `abs.bf16x2` instruction. The native instruction starts at PTX 7.0 / `sm_80`; cuda-oxide admits it from sm_80+. Backend profile floors: LLVM-NVPTX PTX 7.0 on sm_80+; libNVVM PTX 7.0 on sm_80+.
- `abs_f16x2` carries one packed `f16x2` value in a `u32` and lowers to one pure `abs.f16x2` instruction. The native instruction starts at PTX 6.5 / `sm_53`; cuda-oxide admits it from sm_70+. Backend profile floors: LLVM-NVPTX PTX 6.5 on sm_70+; libNVVM PTX 6.5 on sm_75+.
- `add_bf16x2` carries one packed `bf16x2` value in a `u32` and lowers to one pure `add.rn.bf16x2` instruction. The native instruction starts at PTX 7.8 / `sm_90`; cuda-oxide admits it from sm_90+. Backend profile floors: LLVM-NVPTX PTX 7.8 on sm_90+; libNVVM PTX 7.8 on sm_90+.
- `add_f16x2` carries one packed `f16x2` value in a `u32` and lowers to one pure `add.rn.f16x2` instruction. The native instruction starts at PTX 4.2 / `sm_53`; cuda-oxide admits it from sm_70+. Backend profile floors: LLVM-NVPTX PTX 6.0 on sm_70+; libNVVM PTX 4.2 on sm_75+.
- `fma_bf16x2` carries one packed `bf16x2` value in a `u32` and lowers to one pure `fma.rn.bf16x2` instruction. The native instruction starts at PTX 7.0 / `sm_80`; cuda-oxide admits it from sm_80+. Backend profile floors: LLVM-NVPTX PTX 7.0 on sm_80+; libNVVM PTX 7.0 on sm_80+.
- `fma_f16x2` carries one packed `f16x2` value in a `u32` and lowers to one pure `fma.rn.f16x2` instruction. The native instruction starts at PTX 4.2 / `sm_53`; cuda-oxide admits it from sm_70+. Backend profile floors: LLVM-NVPTX PTX 6.0 on sm_70+; libNVVM PTX 4.2 on sm_75+.
- `fma_relu_bf16x2` carries one packed `bf16x2` value in a `u32` and lowers to one pure `fma.rn.relu.bf16x2` instruction. The native instruction starts at PTX 7.0 / `sm_80`; cuda-oxide admits it from sm_80+. Backend profile floors: LLVM-NVPTX PTX 7.0 on sm_80+; libNVVM PTX 7.0 on sm_80+.
- `fma_relu_f16x2` carries one packed `f16x2` value in a `u32` and lowers to one pure `fma.rn.relu.f16x2` instruction. The native instruction starts at PTX 7.0 / `sm_80`; cuda-oxide admits it from sm_80+. Backend profile floors: LLVM-NVPTX PTX 7.0 on sm_80+; libNVVM PTX 7.0 on sm_80+.
- `max_bf16x2` carries one packed `bf16x2` value in a `u32` and lowers to one pure `max.bf16x2` instruction. The native instruction starts at PTX 7.0 / `sm_80`; cuda-oxide admits it from sm_80+. Backend profile floors: LLVM-NVPTX PTX 7.0 on sm_80+; libNVVM PTX 7.0 on sm_80+.
- `max_f16x2` carries one packed `f16x2` value in a `u32` and lowers to one pure `max.f16x2` instruction. The native instruction starts at PTX 7.0 / `sm_80`; cuda-oxide admits it from sm_80+. Backend profile floors: LLVM-NVPTX PTX 7.0 on sm_80+; libNVVM PTX 7.0 on sm_80+.
- `min_bf16x2` carries one packed `bf16x2` value in a `u32` and lowers to one pure `min.bf16x2` instruction. The native instruction starts at PTX 7.0 / `sm_80`; cuda-oxide admits it from sm_80+. Backend profile floors: LLVM-NVPTX PTX 7.0 on sm_80+; libNVVM PTX 7.0 on sm_80+.
- `min_f16x2` carries one packed `f16x2` value in a `u32` and lowers to one pure `min.f16x2` instruction. The native instruction starts at PTX 7.0 / `sm_80`; cuda-oxide admits it from sm_80+. Backend profile floors: LLVM-NVPTX PTX 7.0 on sm_80+; libNVVM PTX 7.0 on sm_80+.
- `mul_bf16x2` carries one packed `bf16x2` value in a `u32` and lowers to one pure `mul.rn.bf16x2` instruction. The native instruction starts at PTX 7.8 / `sm_90`; cuda-oxide admits it from sm_90+. Backend profile floors: LLVM-NVPTX PTX 7.8 on sm_90+; libNVVM PTX 7.8 on sm_90+.
- `mul_f16x2` carries one packed `f16x2` value in a `u32` and lowers to one pure `mul.rn.f16x2` instruction. The native instruction starts at PTX 4.2 / `sm_53`; cuda-oxide admits it from sm_70+. Backend profile floors: LLVM-NVPTX PTX 6.0 on sm_70+; libNVVM PTX 4.2 on sm_75+.
- `neg_bf16x2` carries one packed `bf16x2` value in a `u32` and lowers to one pure `neg.bf16x2` instruction. The native instruction starts at PTX 7.0 / `sm_80`; cuda-oxide admits it from sm_80+. Backend profile floors: LLVM-NVPTX PTX 7.0 on sm_80+; libNVVM PTX 7.0 on sm_80+.
- `neg_f16x2` carries one packed `f16x2` value in a `u32` and lowers to one pure `neg.f16x2` instruction. The native instruction starts at PTX 6.0 / `sm_53`; cuda-oxide admits it from sm_70+. Backend profile floors: LLVM-NVPTX PTX 6.0 on sm_70+; libNVVM PTX 6.0 on sm_75+.
- `sub_bf16x2` carries one packed `bf16x2` value in a `u32` and lowers to one pure `sub.rn.bf16x2` instruction. The native instruction starts at PTX 7.8 / `sm_90`; cuda-oxide admits it from sm_90+. Backend profile floors: LLVM-NVPTX PTX 7.8 on sm_90+; libNVVM PTX 7.8 on sm_90+.
- `sub_f16x2` carries one packed `f16x2` value in a `u32` and lowers to one pure `sub.rn.f16x2` instruction. The native instruction starts at PTX 4.2 / `sm_53`; cuda-oxide admits it from sm_70+. Backend profile floors: LLVM-NVPTX PTX 6.0 on sm_70+; libNVVM PTX 4.2 on sm_75+.

## Packed-conversion contract

- `cvt_f16x2_f32` converts two `f32` inputs to packed `f16x2` using nearest-even rounding without saturation. It lowers to pure `cvt.rn.f16x2.f32` inline PTX. The first input becomes the low lane and the second becomes the high lane, so PTX prints the inputs in reverse order.
- `cvt_f32x2_bf16x2` converts two `f32` inputs to packed `bf16x2` using nearest-even rounding without saturation. It lowers to pure `cvt.rn.bf16x2.f32` inline PTX. The first input becomes the low lane and the second becomes the high lane, so PTX prints the inputs in reverse order.
- `cvt_rn_relu_bf16x2_f32` converts two `f32` inputs to packed `bf16x2` using nearest-even rounding with ReLU. It lowers to pure `cvt.rn.relu.bf16x2.f32` inline PTX. The first input becomes the low lane and the second becomes the high lane, so PTX prints the inputs in reverse order.
- `cvt_rn_relu_f16x2_f32` converts two `f32` inputs to packed `f16x2` using nearest-even rounding with ReLU. It lowers to pure `cvt.rn.relu.f16x2.f32` inline PTX. The first input becomes the low lane and the second becomes the high lane, so PTX prints the inputs in reverse order.
- `cvt_rn_satfinite_e4m3x2_f32` converts two `f32` inputs to packed `e4m3x2` using nearest-even rounding with finite saturation. LLVM-NVPTX uses typed `llvm.nvvm.ff.to.e4m3x2.rn` with `[high, low]` inputs; libNVVM uses pure `cvt.rn.satfinite.e4m3x2.f32` inline PTX. The public first input becomes the low lane and the second becomes the high lane.
- `cvt_rn_satfinite_e5m2x2_f32` converts two `f32` inputs to packed `e5m2x2` using nearest-even rounding with finite saturation. LLVM-NVPTX uses typed `llvm.nvvm.ff.to.e5m2x2.rn` with `[high, low]` inputs; libNVVM uses pure `cvt.rn.satfinite.e5m2x2.f32` inline PTX. The public first input becomes the low lane and the second becomes the high lane.
- `cvt_rn_satfinite_relu_e4m3x2_f32` converts two `f32` inputs to packed `e4m3x2` using nearest-even rounding with finite saturation and ReLU. LLVM-NVPTX uses typed `llvm.nvvm.ff.to.e4m3x2.rn.relu` with `[high, low]` inputs; libNVVM uses pure `cvt.rn.satfinite.relu.e4m3x2.f32` inline PTX. The public first input becomes the low lane and the second becomes the high lane.
- `cvt_rn_satfinite_relu_e5m2x2_f32` converts two `f32` inputs to packed `e5m2x2` using nearest-even rounding with finite saturation and ReLU. LLVM-NVPTX uses typed `llvm.nvvm.ff.to.e5m2x2.rn.relu` with `[high, low]` inputs; libNVVM uses pure `cvt.rn.satfinite.relu.e5m2x2.f32` inline PTX. The public first input becomes the low lane and the second becomes the high lane.
- `cvt_rz_bf16x2_f32` converts two `f32` inputs to packed `bf16x2` using toward-zero rounding without saturation. It lowers to pure `cvt.rz.bf16x2.f32` inline PTX. The first input becomes the low lane and the second becomes the high lane, so PTX prints the inputs in reverse order.
- `cvt_rz_f16x2_f32` converts two `f32` inputs to packed `f16x2` using toward-zero rounding without saturation. It lowers to pure `cvt.rz.f16x2.f32` inline PTX. The first input becomes the low lane and the second becomes the high lane, so PTX prints the inputs in reverse order.

## Scalar TF32-conversion contract

- `cvt_rn_relu_satfinite_tf32_f32` converts one `f32` value to raw TF32 bits with `cvt.rn.relu.satfinite.tf32.f32`. LLVM-NVPTX uses the typed intrinsic; libNVVM uses pure inline PTX so the native target floor is preserved.
- `cvt_rn_relu_tf32_f32` converts one `f32` value to raw TF32 bits with `cvt.rn.relu.tf32.f32`. LLVM-NVPTX uses the typed intrinsic; libNVVM uses pure inline PTX so the native target floor is preserved.
- `cvt_rn_satfinite_tf32_f32` converts one `f32` value to raw TF32 bits with `cvt.rn.satfinite.tf32.f32`. LLVM-NVPTX uses the typed intrinsic; libNVVM uses pure inline PTX so the native target floor is preserved.

- `cvt_rna_satfinite_tf32_f32` converts one `f32` value to raw TF32 bits with `cvt.rna.satfinite.tf32.f32`. LLVM-NVPTX uses the typed intrinsic; libNVVM uses pure inline PTX so the native target floor is preserved.
- `cvt_rna_tf32_f32` converts one `f32` value to raw TF32 bits with `cvt.rna.tf32.f32`. LLVM-NVPTX uses the typed intrinsic; libNVVM uses pure inline PTX so the native target floor is preserved.
- `cvt_rz_relu_satfinite_tf32_f32` converts one `f32` value to raw TF32 bits with `cvt.rz.relu.satfinite.tf32.f32`. LLVM-NVPTX uses the typed intrinsic; libNVVM uses pure inline PTX so the native target floor is preserved.
- `cvt_rz_relu_tf32_f32` converts one `f32` value to raw TF32 bits with `cvt.rz.relu.tf32.f32`. LLVM-NVPTX uses the typed intrinsic; libNVVM uses pure inline PTX so the native target floor is preserved.
- `cvt_rz_satfinite_tf32_f32` converts one `f32` value to raw TF32 bits with `cvt.rz.satfinite.tf32.f32`. LLVM-NVPTX uses the typed intrinsic; libNVVM uses pure inline PTX so the native target floor is preserved.
- `cvt_rz_tf32_f32` converts one `f32` value to raw TF32 bits with `cvt.rz.tf32.f32`. LLVM-NVPTX uses the typed intrinsic; libNVVM uses pure inline PTX so the native target floor is preserved.

## Warp vote contracts

- `all_sync` keeps raw and dialect operands in `[member_mask, predicate]` order. The executing lane must be named in the mask, and every non-exited named lane must execute the same `vote.sync` instruction with the same qualifiers and mask. On `sm_6x` and earlier, all named lanes must execute in convergence and no unnamed lane may be active. Both immediate and register member masks are admitted.
- `any_sync` keeps raw and dialect operands in `[member_mask, predicate]` order. The executing lane must be named in the mask, and every non-exited named lane must execute the same `vote.sync` instruction with the same qualifiers and mask. On `sm_6x` and earlier, all named lanes must execute in convergence and no unnamed lane may be active. Both immediate and register member masks are admitted.
- `ballot_sync` keeps raw and dialect operands in `[member_mask, predicate]` order. The executing lane must be named in the mask, and every non-exited named lane must execute the same `vote.sync` instruction with the same qualifiers and mask. On `sm_6x` and earlier, all named lanes must execute in convergence and no unnamed lane may be active. Both immediate and register member masks are admitted.
- `uni_sync` keeps raw and dialect operands in `[member_mask, predicate]` order. The executing lane must be named in the mask, and every non-exited named lane must execute the same `vote.sync` instruction with the same qualifiers and mask. On `sm_6x` and earlier, all named lanes must execute in convergence and no unnamed lane may be active. Both immediate and register member masks are admitted.

## Active-mask contract

- `active_mask` observes the lanes active at the instruction. LLVM uses the typed intrinsic; libNVVM uses reviewed convergent, side-effecting inline PTX because that backend does not select the intrinsic.

## Warp-match contracts

- `match_all_i64_sync` keeps operands in `[member_mask, value]` order and projects field 0 from LLVM's `{i32, i1}` result. The executing lane must be named in the mask, and every non-exited named lane must execute the same `match.sync` operation with the same qualifiers and mask. All register/immediate value and mask forms are admitted.
- `match_all_sync` keeps operands in `[member_mask, value]` order and projects field 0 from LLVM's `{i32, i1}` result. The executing lane must be named in the mask, and every non-exited named lane must execute the same `match.sync` operation with the same qualifiers and mask. All register/immediate value and mask forms are admitted.
- `match_any_i64_sync` keeps operands in `[member_mask, value]` order and returns LLVM's mask directly. The executing lane must be named in the mask, and every non-exited named lane must execute the same `match.sync` operation with the same qualifiers and mask. All register/immediate value and mask forms are admitted.
- `match_any_sync` keeps operands in `[member_mask, value]` order and returns LLVM's mask directly. The executing lane must be named in the mask, and every non-exited named lane must execute the same `match.sync` operation with the same qualifiers and mask. All register/immediate value and mask forms are admitted.

## Warp-barrier contract

- `sync_mask` passes the 32-bit member mask directly to the typed LLVM intrinsic on both backends. The executing lane must be named in the mask, and every non-exited named lane must execute the same `bar.warp.sync` operation with the same mask. On `sm_6x` and earlier, all named lanes must execute the barrier in convergence, and no unnamed lane may be active when it executes. The barrier orders memory accesses among participating lanes. Both immediate and register masks are admitted.

## Warp-shuffle contracts

- `shuffle_down_f32_sync` keeps raw and dialect operands in `[member_mask, value, lane_or_delta]` order and inserts clamp `31` during lowering. The executing lane must be named in the mask, and every non-exited named lane must execute the same `shfl.sync` operation with the same qualifiers and mask. On `sm_6x` and earlier, all named lanes must execute in convergence and no unnamed lane may be active. A computed in-range source must be active and named; if PTX marks it out of range, the calling lane's input is copied. Register and immediate lane/mask forms are admitted.
- `shuffle_down_sync` keeps raw and dialect operands in `[member_mask, value, lane_or_delta]` order and inserts clamp `31` during lowering. The executing lane must be named in the mask, and every non-exited named lane must execute the same `shfl.sync` operation with the same qualifiers and mask. On `sm_6x` and earlier, all named lanes must execute in convergence and no unnamed lane may be active. A computed in-range source must be active and named; if PTX marks it out of range, the calling lane's input is copied. Register and immediate lane/mask forms are admitted.
- `shuffle_down_u64_sync` keeps raw and dialect operands in `[member_mask, value, lane_or_delta]` order and inserts clamp `31` during lowering. The executing lane must be named in the mask, and every non-exited named lane must execute the same `shfl.sync` operation with the same qualifiers and mask. On `sm_6x` and earlier, all named lanes must execute in convergence and no unnamed lane may be active. A computed in-range source must be active and named; if PTX marks it out of range, the calling lane's input is copied. One convergent, side-effecting inline-PTX block splits `i64`, performs low then high `b32` shuffles with the same register lane/mask, and reassembles it.
- `shuffle_f32_sync` keeps raw and dialect operands in `[member_mask, value, lane_or_delta]` order and inserts clamp `31` during lowering. The executing lane must be named in the mask, and every non-exited named lane must execute the same `shfl.sync` operation with the same qualifiers and mask. On `sm_6x` and earlier, all named lanes must execute in convergence and no unnamed lane may be active. A computed in-range source must be active and named; if PTX marks it out of range, the calling lane's input is copied. Register and immediate lane/mask forms are admitted.
- `shuffle_sync` keeps raw and dialect operands in `[member_mask, value, lane_or_delta]` order and inserts clamp `31` during lowering. The executing lane must be named in the mask, and every non-exited named lane must execute the same `shfl.sync` operation with the same qualifiers and mask. On `sm_6x` and earlier, all named lanes must execute in convergence and no unnamed lane may be active. A computed in-range source must be active and named; if PTX marks it out of range, the calling lane's input is copied. Register and immediate lane/mask forms are admitted.
- `shuffle_u64_sync` keeps raw and dialect operands in `[member_mask, value, lane_or_delta]` order and inserts clamp `31` during lowering. The executing lane must be named in the mask, and every non-exited named lane must execute the same `shfl.sync` operation with the same qualifiers and mask. On `sm_6x` and earlier, all named lanes must execute in convergence and no unnamed lane may be active. A computed in-range source must be active and named; if PTX marks it out of range, the calling lane's input is copied. One convergent, side-effecting inline-PTX block splits `i64`, performs low then high `b32` shuffles with the same register lane/mask, and reassembles it.
- `shuffle_up_f32_sync` keeps raw and dialect operands in `[member_mask, value, lane_or_delta]` order and inserts clamp `0` during lowering. The executing lane must be named in the mask, and every non-exited named lane must execute the same `shfl.sync` operation with the same qualifiers and mask. On `sm_6x` and earlier, all named lanes must execute in convergence and no unnamed lane may be active. A computed in-range source must be active and named; if PTX marks it out of range, the calling lane's input is copied. Register and immediate lane/mask forms are admitted.
- `shuffle_up_sync` keeps raw and dialect operands in `[member_mask, value, lane_or_delta]` order and inserts clamp `0` during lowering. The executing lane must be named in the mask, and every non-exited named lane must execute the same `shfl.sync` operation with the same qualifiers and mask. On `sm_6x` and earlier, all named lanes must execute in convergence and no unnamed lane may be active. A computed in-range source must be active and named; if PTX marks it out of range, the calling lane's input is copied. Register and immediate lane/mask forms are admitted.
- `shuffle_up_u64_sync` keeps raw and dialect operands in `[member_mask, value, lane_or_delta]` order and inserts clamp `0` during lowering. The executing lane must be named in the mask, and every non-exited named lane must execute the same `shfl.sync` operation with the same qualifiers and mask. On `sm_6x` and earlier, all named lanes must execute in convergence and no unnamed lane may be active. A computed in-range source must be active and named; if PTX marks it out of range, the calling lane's input is copied. One convergent, side-effecting inline-PTX block splits `i64`, performs low then high `b32` shuffles with the same register lane/mask, and reassembles it.
- `shuffle_xor_f32_sync` keeps raw and dialect operands in `[member_mask, value, lane_or_delta]` order and inserts clamp `31` during lowering. The executing lane must be named in the mask, and every non-exited named lane must execute the same `shfl.sync` operation with the same qualifiers and mask. On `sm_6x` and earlier, all named lanes must execute in convergence and no unnamed lane may be active. A computed in-range source must be active and named; if PTX marks it out of range, the calling lane's input is copied. Register and immediate lane/mask forms are admitted.
- `shuffle_xor_sync` keeps raw and dialect operands in `[member_mask, value, lane_or_delta]` order and inserts clamp `31` during lowering. The executing lane must be named in the mask, and every non-exited named lane must execute the same `shfl.sync` operation with the same qualifiers and mask. On `sm_6x` and earlier, all named lanes must execute in convergence and no unnamed lane may be active. A computed in-range source must be active and named; if PTX marks it out of range, the calling lane's input is copied. Register and immediate lane/mask forms are admitted.
- `shuffle_xor_u64_sync` keeps raw and dialect operands in `[member_mask, value, lane_or_delta]` order and inserts clamp `31` during lowering. The executing lane must be named in the mask, and every non-exited named lane must execute the same `shfl.sync` operation with the same qualifiers and mask. On `sm_6x` and earlier, all named lanes must execute in convergence and no unnamed lane may be active. A computed in-range source must be active and named; if PTX marks it out of range, the calling lane's input is copied. One convergent, side-effecting inline-PTX block splits `i64`, performs low then high `b32` shuffles with the same register lane/mask, and reassembles it.

## Cluster-barrier contracts

- `barrier_cluster_arrive` has release ordering. Each non-exited cluster thread must arrive exactly once before completion, then execute the matching wait. The instruction is not aligned.
- `barrier_cluster_arrive_aligned` has release ordering. Each non-exited cluster thread must arrive exactly once before completion, then execute the matching wait. Every non-exited warp thread must execute the same aligned instruction in identical control flow.
- `barrier_cluster_arrive_relaxed` has relaxed ordering. Each non-exited cluster thread must arrive exactly once before completion, then execute the matching wait. The instruction is not aligned.
- `barrier_cluster_arrive_relaxed_aligned` has relaxed ordering. Each non-exited cluster thread must arrive exactly once before completion, then execute the matching wait. Every non-exited warp thread must execute the same aligned instruction in identical control flow.
- `barrier_cluster_wait` has acquire ordering. Each non-exited cluster thread must arrive exactly once before completion, then execute the matching wait. The instruction is not aligned.
- `barrier_cluster_wait_aligned` has acquire ordering. Each non-exited cluster thread must arrive exactly once before completion, then execute the matching wait. Every non-exited warp thread must execute the same aligned instruction in identical control flow.
- `cuda_device::cluster::cluster_sync` is the generated compatibility operation: aligned arrive followed by aligned wait.

## Cluster-memory contracts

- `dsmem_read_u32` has no one-to-one LLVM intrinsic. Both backends use one convergent `mapa.shared::cluster.u64` plus `ld.shared::cluster.u32` block with a compiler memory clobber.
- `map_shared_rank` keeps LLVM 22's address-space-7 result as source identity only. Both backends use exact convergent `mapa.shared::cluster.u64` inline PTX and preserve the established shared-address carrier; an ordinary pointer dereference is not a remote cluster load.

## movmatrix contracts

- `movmatrix_trans_b16` transposes one packed b16 fragment in registers. All warp lanes must execute the same instruction, and no lane may have exited.

## Extended mbarrier contracts

- `fence_mbarrier_init_release_cluster` keeps its established CUDA-device signature and exact convergent inline-PTX memory clobber. Its source identity is LLVM 22 TableGen.
- `fence_proxy_async_generic_acquire_shared_cluster_cluster` keeps its established CUDA-device signature and exact convergent inline-PTX memory clobber. Its source identity is LLVM 22 TableGen.
- `fence_proxy_async_generic_release_shared_cta_cluster` keeps its established CUDA-device signature and exact convergent inline-PTX memory clobber. Its source identity is LLVM 22 TableGen.
- `fence_proxy_async_shared_cta` keeps its established CUDA-device signature and exact convergent inline-PTX memory clobber. Its source identity is LLVM 22 TableGen.
- `mbarrier_arrive_cluster` keeps its established CUDA-device signature and exact convergent inline-PTX memory clobber. Its source identity is PTX-native raw-address contract.
- `mbarrier_arrive_expect_tx` keeps its established CUDA-device signature and exact convergent inline-PTX memory clobber. Its source identity is LLVM 22 TableGen.
- `mbarrier_arrive_expect_tx_cluster` keeps its established CUDA-device signature and exact convergent inline-PTX memory clobber. Its source identity is LLVM 22 TableGen.
- `mbarrier_try_wait` keeps its established CUDA-device signature and exact convergent inline-PTX memory clobber. Its source identity is LLVM 22 TableGen.
- `mbarrier_try_wait_parity` keeps its established CUDA-device signature and exact convergent inline-PTX memory clobber. Its source identity is LLVM 22 TableGen.
- `mbarrier_try_wait_parity_cluster` keeps its established CUDA-device signature and exact convergent inline-PTX memory clobber. Its source identity is LLVM 22 TableGen.
- `nanosleep` keeps its established CUDA-device signature and exact convergent inline-PTX memory clobber. Its source identity is LLVM 22 TableGen.

## WGMMA-control contracts

- `wgmma_commit_group` must be executed by all four warps in the warpgroup with the same control flow. It commits the warpgroup's prior uncommitted WGMMA operations.
- `wgmma_fence` must be executed by all four warps in the warpgroup with the same control flow. It orders register writes before later WGMMA operations.
- `wgmma_wait_group` must be executed by all four warps in the warpgroup with the same control flow. Its `u64` argument must be a compile-time constant.

## Synchronization contracts

- `sync_threads` inserts the fixed barrier ID `0`. Every active CTA thread must reach the same barrier; divergent use can deadlock the CTA.
- `threadfence` emits `membar.gl` through the reviewed typed NVVM route on both backends.
- `threadfence_block` emits `membar.cta` through the reviewed typed NVVM route on both backends.
- `threadfence_system` emits `membar.sys` through the reviewed typed NVVM route on both backends.

## cp.async mbarrier contracts

- `cp_async_mbarrier_arrive` uses generic addressing and increments the pending count before the later asynchronous decrement. It associates only the executing thread's prior `cp.async` operations with a live, eight-byte-aligned shared-memory barrier. LLVM-NVPTX uses the typed intrinsic; libNVVM uses reviewed convergent, side-effecting inline PTX with a memory clobber.
- `cp_async_mbarrier_arrive_noinc` uses generic addressing and does not increment the pending count, so initialization must already include the later asynchronous decrement. It associates only the executing thread's prior `cp.async` operations with a live, eight-byte-aligned shared-memory barrier. LLVM-NVPTX uses the typed intrinsic; libNVVM uses reviewed convergent, side-effecting inline PTX with a memory clobber.
- `cp_async_mbarrier_arrive_noinc_shared` uses explicit shared addressing and does not increment the pending count, so initialization must already include the later asynchronous decrement. It associates only the executing thread's prior `cp.async` operations with a live, eight-byte-aligned shared-memory barrier. LLVM-NVPTX uses the typed intrinsic; libNVVM uses reviewed convergent, side-effecting inline PTX with a memory clobber.
- `cp_async_mbarrier_arrive_shared` uses explicit shared addressing and increments the pending count before the later asynchronous decrement. It associates only the executing thread's prior `cp.async` operations with a live, eight-byte-aligned shared-memory barrier. LLVM-NVPTX uses the typed intrinsic; libNVVM uses reviewed convergent, side-effecting inline PTX with a memory clobber.

## Basic mbarrier contracts

- `mbarrier_arrive` records one expected arrival and returns a token for the same barrier phase. It lowers through the typed NVVM intrinsic with LLVM-NVPTX and inline PTX with libNVVM; generic MIR pointers are normalized to shared address space during lowering.
- `mbarrier_init` initializes one eight-byte-aligned shared-memory object exactly once with an expected count in `1..=0xFFFFF`. It lowers through the typed NVVM intrinsic with LLVM-NVPTX and inline PTX with libNVVM; generic MIR pointers are normalized to shared address space during lowering.
- `mbarrier_inval` invalidates an initialized barrier only after all users are finished. It lowers through the typed NVVM intrinsic with LLVM-NVPTX and inline PTX with libNVVM; generic MIR pointers are normalized to shared address space during lowering.
- `mbarrier_test_wait` tests one token from the same barrier phase with convergent, side-effecting inline PTX. It lowers through inline PTX on both backends; generic MIR pointers are normalized to shared address space during lowering.

- `wgmma_fence`: properties `IntrConvergent`; result `NoUndef` false; half-open result range `none`; selection records `WGMMA_FENCE_SYNC_ALIGNED`; selection predicates `Subtarget->getPTXVersion() >= 80`, `hasSM90a`
- `wgmma_wait_group`: properties `ImmArg<arg0>`, `IntrConvergent`; result `NoUndef` false; half-open result range `none`; selection records `WGMMA_WAIT_GROUP_SYNC_ALIGNED`; selection predicates `Subtarget->getPTXVersion() >= 80`, `hasSM90a`

- `abs_bf16x2`: runtime `not recorded`

    - PTX assembly on `sm_80, ptx70`: `succeeded` — CUDA 13.3 ptxas accepted the 1,792-byte PTX 7.0 module for sm_80 and produced a 3,624-byte cubin (SHA-256 0a1a7b10c48e32f35b3cdce0f228a2fdf5d964570c023f3bbd34a09f1ebb7d9d). Tool `/usr/local/cuda-13.3/bin/ptxas` reports `CUDA 13.3 V13.3.33 build cuda_13.3.r13.3/compiler.37862127_0` (SHA-256 `7fdd01a4cf50e30746da98989c9272a907f491e6fd7fecfda14642e4375f88fb`).
  - `lib_nvvm` uses `inline_ptx` from profile `cuda-13.3-libnvvm-13.3.33` at PTX 7.0 / sm_80+: status `validated` (`CUDA toolkit 13.3; nvcc/libNVVM release V13.3.33`, SHA-256 `b6a5db233133fb0ed693f8cb8aeb1d405254ce0ab10364478985292ec98cfcf0`)
    - backend codegen on `sm_80, ptx93`: `succeeded` — CUDA 13.3 libNVVM accepted the exact abs.bf16x2 route at compute_80 as part of the combined seven-form probe and produced 2,256-byte LTOIR (SHA-256 a9394a9ba0813c36f65bfc2636d7e80b27ab2ffcb6ded14a420d4127a824028d).
    - device link on `sm_80, ptx93`: `succeeded` — nvJitLink produced a 3,624-byte sm_80 cubin (SHA-256 1c8ff2eeb9e58f8b3defc22ed929cd48ec2e2f2beab30747f937a9a7869d74d3); cuobjdump showed HFMA2.BF16_V2, HFMA2.BF16_V2.RELU, HMNMX2.BF16_V2, sign/absolute HFMA2 forms, and F2FP.BF16.PACK_AB. Tool `/usr/local/cuda-13.3/targets/x86_64-linux/lib/libnvJitLink.so.13.3.33` reports `V13.3.33` (SHA-256 `3ba1e744347cd68617b862eccfd98b125482e882b7a6319f42abc9a768513db8`). Artifact `cubin`.
- `abs_f16x2`: runtime `not recorded`
  - `llvm_nvptx` uses `inline_ptx` from profile `rust-llvm-22.1.2-1cb4e383` at PTX 6.5 / sm_70+: status `validated` (`LLVM version 22.1.2-rust-1.96.0-nightly`, SHA-256 `ff1dcdc18a5441003d43c5df267b43e2b6dd3efccca38c20bb2801885626331e`)
    - backend codegen on `sm_70, ptx65`: `succeeded` — The selected Rust LLVM 22.1.2 backend retained exact abs.f16x2 inline PTX at PTX 6.5/sm_70. An independent typed call to the resolved llvm.nvvm.fabs.v2f16 symbol emitted the same instruction at PTX 6.5/sm_70 and at its native sm_53 floor; sm_52 failed exact intrinsic selection.
    - PTX assembly on `sm_75, ptx65`: `succeeded` — CUDA 13.3 ptxas accepted the 737-byte PTX module at sm_75 and produced a 3,112-byte cubin (SHA-256 24150b058e7b7ad99ffeacd77588a2f1febdf5479f93f966ac0192c9834babf7). SASS realizes packed absolute value as HADD2 with an absolute source. The PTX 6.4 negative module was rejected because abs.f16/abs.f16x2 requires PTX 6.5 or later. Tool `/usr/local/cuda-13.3/bin/ptxas` reports `CUDA 13.3 V13.3.33 build cuda_13.3.r13.3/compiler.37862127_0` (SHA-256 `7fdd01a4cf50e30746da98989c9272a907f491e6fd7fecfda14642e4375f88fb`).
  - `lib_nvvm` uses `inline_ptx` from profile `cuda-13.3-libnvvm-13.3.33` at PTX 6.5 / sm_75+: status `validated` (`CUDA toolkit 13.3; nvcc/libNVVM release V13.3.33`, SHA-256 `b6a5db233133fb0ed693f8cb8aeb1d405254ce0ab10364478985292ec98cfcf0`)
    - backend codegen on `sm_75, ptx93`: `succeeded` — CUDA 13.3 libNVVM accepted exact abs.f16x2 at compute_75 and produced 1,648-byte LTOIR (SHA-256 e26755d764c7c6576f9bd387d573a868fa247ad1b2c900ad7ab8b8486f1fc79c). The instruction floor is PTX 6.5/sm_53; compute_75 is the CUDA 13.3 profile floor.
    - device link on `sm_75, ptx93`: `succeeded` — nvJitLink produced a 3,112-byte sm_75 cubin (SHA-256 e780ddaee22735b1c97e35157fae73685685268a445eef4a890d805bb6958c37). cuobjdump shows the same absolute-source HADD2 form as the LLVM/ptxas path. Tool `/usr/local/cuda-13.3/lib64/libnvJitLink.so.13.3.33` reports `V13.3.33` (SHA-256 `3ba1e744347cd68617b862eccfd98b125482e882b7a6319f42abc9a768513db8`). Artifact `cubin`.

- `cvt_f16x2_f32`: runtime `not recorded`
  - `llvm_nvptx` uses `inline_ptx` from profile `rust-llvm-22.1.2-1cb4e383` at PTX 7.0 / sm_80+: status `validated` (`LLVM version 22.1.2-rust-1.96.0-nightly`, SHA-256 `ff1dcdc18a5441003d43c5df267b43e2b6dd3efccca38c20bb2801885626331e`)
    - declaration canonicalization on `sm_80`: `succeeded` — The selected Rust LLVM 22.1.2 backend canonicalized this declaration to nocallback, nofree, nosync, nounwind, speculatable, willreturn, and memory(none) in the combined 2,683-byte canonical IR artifact (SHA-256 172ae44a3b3f55425bb296f9f485ea08fe9f51dada6721d5a5fbf8b61cfd6d60).
    - backend codegen on `sm_80, ptx70`: `succeeded` — The selected Rust LLVM 22.1.2 backend emitted exact cvt.rn.f16x2.f32 once in the combined 1,286-byte typed PTX artifact (SHA-256 263e8020dcdc7ccbb25baeefba06104abde3cac5100e98d40f9304746992ef97). Passing high then low preserves the public first-argument-low lane convention.
    - PTX assembly on `sm_80, ptx70`: `succeeded` — CUDA 13.3 ptxas accepted the combined 1,286-byte typed PTX module at sm_80 and produced a 3,624-byte cubin (SHA-256 f12457bfc7cc44d96014d876f4705a6ddee14db8471828df74dec91d060ff929). cuobjdump contains all six distinct F2FP packed-conversion forms. Tool `/usr/local/cuda-13.3/bin/ptxas` reports `CUDA 13.3 V13.3.33 build cuda_13.3.r13.3/compiler.37862127_0` (SHA-256 `7fdd01a4cf50e30746da98989c9272a907f491e6fd7fecfda14642e4375f88fb`). Artifact `cubin`.
    - backend codegen on `sm_80, ptx70`: `succeeded` — The selected Rust LLVM 22.1.2 backend retained exact cvt.rn.f16x2.f32 in the combined six-form production probe, with the public low/high arguments printed as PTX high/low. An independent typed call to llvm.nvvm.ff2f16x2.rn, invoked with the kernel high value followed by the low value, emitted the same exact instruction and PTX operand order even though the normalized imported record has no named selections.
    - PTX assembly on `sm_80, ptx70`: `succeeded` — CUDA 13.3 ptxas accepted the 1,462-byte PTX 7.0 module at sm_80 and produced a 3,496-byte cubin (SHA-256 c92d92589eadec753288e74985a1775e3f822765bffb6a88746c4a31ccaf7b6d). cuobjdump contains all six distinct F2FP packed-conversion forms. PTX 6.5 rejected every form as requiring PTX 7.0; sm_75 rejected every form as requiring sm_80. Tool `/usr/local/cuda-13.3/bin/ptxas` reports `CUDA 13.3 V13.3.33 build cuda_13.3.r13.3/compiler.37862127_0` (SHA-256 `7fdd01a4cf50e30746da98989c9272a907f491e6fd7fecfda14642e4375f88fb`).
  - `lib_nvvm` uses `inline_ptx` from profile `cuda-13.3-libnvvm-13.3.33` at PTX 7.0 / sm_80+: status `validated` (`CUDA toolkit 13.3; nvcc/libNVVM release V13.3.33`, SHA-256 `b6a5db233133fb0ed693f8cb8aeb1d405254ce0ab10364478985292ec98cfcf0`)
    - backend codegen on `sm_80, ptx93`: `succeeded` — CUDA 13.3 libNVVM accepted exact cvt.rn.f16x2.f32 at compute_80 as part of the combined six-form inline-PTX probe and produced 2,164-byte LTOIR (SHA-256 a0617e3e49b1df2beb5f575ae8750e0b62a57ef403db4b793dc77b5e1ef715f7). This proves only the production inline-PTX route; it does not claim typed libNVVM support.
    - device link on `sm_80, ptx93`: `succeeded` — nvJitLink produced a 3,496-byte sm_80 cubin (SHA-256 95af39e283c3ad359158c7ed7dcca98a43ebfac1a6a7c49f7c10081224218726). cuobjdump shows the same six F2FP forms as the LLVM/ptxas path. A compute_75 LTOIR reached nvJitLink but failed terminal PTX compilation because every form requires sm_80. Tool `/usr/local/cuda-13.3/lib64/libnvJitLink.so.13.3.33` reports `V13.3.33` (SHA-256 `3ba1e744347cd68617b862eccfd98b125482e882b7a6319f42abc9a768513db8`). Artifact `cubin`.

- `cvt_rz_bf16x2_f32`: runtime `not recorded`
  - `llvm_nvptx` uses `inline_ptx` from profile `rust-llvm-22.1.2-1cb4e383` at PTX 7.0 / sm_80+: status `validated` (`LLVM version 22.1.2-rust-1.96.0-nightly`, SHA-256 `ff1dcdc18a5441003d43c5df267b43e2b6dd3efccca38c20bb2801885626331e`)
    - declaration canonicalization on `sm_80`: `succeeded` — The selected Rust LLVM 22.1.2 backend canonicalized this declaration to nocallback, nofree, nosync, nounwind, speculatable, willreturn, and memory(none) in the combined 2,683-byte canonical IR artifact (SHA-256 172ae44a3b3f55425bb296f9f485ea08fe9f51dada6721d5a5fbf8b61cfd6d60).
    - backend codegen on `sm_80, ptx70`: `succeeded` — The selected Rust LLVM 22.1.2 backend emitted exact cvt.rz.bf16x2.f32 once in the combined 1,286-byte typed PTX artifact (SHA-256 263e8020dcdc7ccbb25baeefba06104abde3cac5100e98d40f9304746992ef97). Passing high then low preserves the public first-argument-low lane convention.
    - PTX assembly on `sm_80, ptx70`: `succeeded` — CUDA 13.3 ptxas accepted the combined 1,286-byte typed PTX module at sm_80 and produced a 3,624-byte cubin (SHA-256 f12457bfc7cc44d96014d876f4705a6ddee14db8471828df74dec91d060ff929). cuobjdump contains all six distinct F2FP packed-conversion forms. Tool `/usr/local/cuda-13.3/bin/ptxas` reports `CUDA 13.3 V13.3.33 build cuda_13.3.r13.3/compiler.37862127_0` (SHA-256 `7fdd01a4cf50e30746da98989c9272a907f491e6fd7fecfda14642e4375f88fb`). Artifact `cubin`.
    - backend codegen on `sm_80, ptx70`: `succeeded` — The selected Rust LLVM 22.1.2 backend retained exact cvt.rz.bf16x2.f32 in the combined six-form production probe, with the public low/high arguments printed as PTX high/low. An independent typed call to llvm.nvvm.ff2bf16x2.rz, invoked with the kernel high value followed by the low value, emitted the same exact instruction and PTX operand order even though the normalized imported record has no named selections.
    - PTX assembly on `sm_80, ptx70`: `succeeded` — CUDA 13.3 ptxas accepted the 1,462-byte PTX 7.0 module at sm_80 and produced a 3,496-byte cubin (SHA-256 c92d92589eadec753288e74985a1775e3f822765bffb6a88746c4a31ccaf7b6d). cuobjdump contains all six distinct F2FP packed-conversion forms. PTX 6.5 rejected every form as requiring PTX 7.0; sm_75 rejected every form as requiring sm_80. Tool `/usr/local/cuda-13.3/bin/ptxas` reports `CUDA 13.3 V13.3.33 build cuda_13.3.r13.3/compiler.37862127_0` (SHA-256 `7fdd01a4cf50e30746da98989c9272a907f491e6fd7fecfda14642e4375f88fb`).
  - `lib_nvvm` uses `inline_ptx` from profile `cuda-13.3-libnvvm-13.3.33` at PTX 7.0 / sm_80+: status `validated` (`CUDA toolkit 13.3; nvcc/libNVVM release V13.3.33`, SHA-256 `b6a5db233133fb0ed693f8cb8aeb1d405254ce0ab10364478985292ec98cfcf0`)
    - backend codegen on `sm_80, ptx93`: `succeeded` — CUDA 13.3 libNVVM accepted exact cvt.rz.bf16x2.f32 at compute_80 as part of the combined six-form inline-PTX probe and produced 2,164-byte LTOIR (SHA-256 a0617e3e49b1df2beb5f575ae8750e0b62a57ef403db4b793dc77b5e1ef715f7). This proves only the production inline-PTX route; it does not claim typed libNVVM support.
    - device link on `sm_80, ptx93`: `succeeded` — nvJitLink produced a 3,496-byte sm_80 cubin (SHA-256 95af39e283c3ad359158c7ed7dcca98a43ebfac1a6a7c49f7c10081224218726). cuobjdump shows the same six F2FP forms as the LLVM/ptxas path. A compute_75 LTOIR reached nvJitLink but failed terminal PTX compilation because every form requires sm_80. Tool `/usr/local/cuda-13.3/lib64/libnvJitLink.so.13.3.33` reports `V13.3.33` (SHA-256 `3ba1e744347cd68617b862eccfd98b125482e882b7a6319f42abc9a768513db8`). Artifact `cubin`.

An independent typed call to llvm.nvvm.fma.rn.f16x2 emitted the same instruction at PTX 6.0/sm_70 and at its native PTX 4.2/sm_53 floor; sm_52 failed exact intrinsic selection.
    - PTX assembly on `sm_75, ptx60`: `succeeded` — CUDA 13.3 ptxas accepted the 1,252-byte PTX 6.0 module at sm_75 and produced a 3,368-byte cubin (SHA-256 3f23db63edf3340a638d464a8423346292f17846bed5e8c921d01c03371a8633). SASS contains HFMA2, HADD2 add/sub forms, and HMUL2. The PTX 4.1 negative module was rejected for every instruction as requiring PTX 4.2 or later. Tool `/usr/local/cuda-13.3/bin/ptxas` reports `CUDA 13.3 V13.3.33 build cuda_13.3.r13.3/compiler.37862127_0` (SHA-256 `7fdd01a4cf50e30746da98989c9272a907f491e6fd7fecfda14642e4375f88fb`).

    - backend codegen on `sm_75, ptx93`: `succeeded` — CUDA 13.3 libNVVM accepted the exact route at compute_75 as part of the combined four-form probe and produced 1,932-byte LTOIR (SHA-256 edb864f3999af5c06558f9dde87cd506ac0c2188cb6a71b19fbf8b33d6b5e5ae). The instruction floor is PTX 4.2/sm_53; compute_75 is this libNVVM profile's floor, and compute_70 is rejected as an unsupported profile option.
    - device link on `sm_75, ptx93`: `succeeded` — nvJitLink produced a 3,368-byte sm_75 cubin (SHA-256 5ca3d1ca1d9c56802bb78bdad8f5e1138b17dc4dc9f96a7b1e9572eb4010d50c). cuobjdump SASS contains HFMA2, HADD2 add/sub forms, and HMUL2, matching the LLVM/ptxas path.   - `llvm_nvptx` uses `typed_nvvm` from profile `rust-llvm-22.1.2-1cb4e383-blackwell-ldmatrix` at PTX 8.6 / sm_100a or sm_100f or sm_103a or sm_103f or sm_110a or sm_110f or sm_120a or sm_120f or sm_121a or sm_121f: status `validated` (`LLVM version 22.1.2-rust-1.96.0-nightly`, SHA-256 `ff1dcdc18a5441003d43c5df267b43e2b6dd3efccca38c20bb2801885626331e`)

Literal _p64 and _p32 remain part of the LLVM symbol before the .p3 suffix. sm_101a at PTX 8.6 and sm_101f at PTX 8.8 emit successfully, but both abort instruction selection at PTX 9.0; the minimum-only catalog therefore excludes both non-monotonic spellings.

- `ldmatrix_m8n16_x1_b8x16_b4x16_p64`: runtime `unexecuted`
  - `llvm_nvptx` uses `typed_nvvm` from profile `rust-llvm-22.1.2-1cb4e383-blackwell-ldmatrix` at PTX 8.6 / sm_100a or sm_100f or sm_103a or sm_103f or sm_110a or sm_110f or sm_120a or sm_120f or sm_121a or sm_121f: status `validated` (`LLVM version 22.1.2-rust-1.96.0-nightly`, SHA-256 `ff1dcdc18a5441003d43c5df267b43e2b6dd3efccca38c20bb2801885626331e`)
    - declaration canonicalization on `sm_100a, ptx86`: `succeeded` — Pinned LLVM 22 recognized every exact .p3 declaration and its imported convergent readonly contract in /tmp/blackwell-ldmatrix-probe.ll (4,153 bytes; SHA-256 1fac276e40ad836eb70cc7b8001adbf31fb29e1ef3791c95917cf878b2b8d211). Literal _p64 and _p32 remain part of the LLVM symbol before the .p3 suffix. sm_101a at PTX 8.6 and sm_101f at PTX 8.8 emit successfully, but both abort instruction selection at PTX 9.0; the minimum-only catalog therefore excludes both non-monotonic spellings.
    - backend codegen on `sm_100a, ptx86`: `succeeded` — Command: llc -O0 -march=nvptx64 -mcpu=sm_100a -mattr=+ptx86 /tmp/blackwell-ldmatrix-probe.ll -o /tmp/audit-bw-min-sm_100a-ptx86.ptx.   - `llvm_nvptx` uses `typed_nvvm` from profile `rust-llvm-22.1.2-1cb4e383-blackwell-ldmatrix` at PTX 8.6 / sm_100a or sm_100f or sm_103a or sm_103f or sm_110a or sm_110f or sm_120a or sm_120f or sm_121a or sm_121f: status `validated` (`LLVM version 22.1.2-rust-1.96.0-nightly`, SHA-256 `ff1dcdc18a5441003d43c5df267b43e2b6dd3efccca38c20bb2801885626331e`)
    - declaration canonicalization on `sm_100a, ptx86`: `succeeded` — Pinned LLVM 22 recognized every exact .p3 declaration and its imported convergent readonly contract in /tmp/blackwell-ldmatrix-probe.ll (4,153 bytes; SHA-256 1fac276e40ad836eb70cc7b8001adbf31fb29e1ef3791c95917cf878b2b8d211). Literal _p64 and _p32 remain part of the LLVM symbol before the .p3 suffix. sm_101a at PTX 8.6 and sm_101f at PTX 8.8 emit successfully, but both abort instruction selection at PTX 9.0; the minimum-only catalog therefore excludes both non-monotonic spellings.

  - `llvm_nvptx` uses `typed_nvvm` from profile `rust-llvm-22.1.2-1cb4e383-blackwell-ldmatrix` at PTX 8.6 / sm_100a or sm_100f or sm_103a or sm_103f or sm_110a or sm_110f or sm_120a or sm_120f or sm_121a or sm_121f: status `validated` (`LLVM version 22.1.2-rust-1.96.0-nightly`, SHA-256 `ff1dcdc18a5441003d43c5df267b43e2b6dd3efccca38c20bb2801885626331e`)

Literal _p64 and _p32 remain part of the LLVM symbol before the .p3 suffix. sm_101a at PTX 8.6 and sm_101f at PTX 8.8 emit successfully, but both abort instruction selection at PTX 9.0; the minimum-only catalog therefore excludes both non-monotonic spellings.

- `ldmatrix_m8n16_x4_b8x16_b4x16_p64`: runtime `unexecuted`
  - `llvm_nvptx` uses `typed_nvvm` from profile `rust-llvm-22.1.2-1cb4e383-blackwell-ldmatrix` at PTX 8.6 / sm_100a or sm_100f or sm_103a or sm_103f or sm_110a or sm_110f or sm_120a or sm_120f or sm_121a or sm_121f: status `validated` (`LLVM version 22.1.2-rust-1.96.0-nightly`, SHA-256 `ff1dcdc18a5441003d43c5df267b43e2b6dd3efccca38c20bb2801885626331e`)
    - declaration canonicalization on `sm_100a, ptx86`: `succeeded` — Pinned LLVM 22 recognized every exact .p3 declaration and its imported convergent readonly contract in /tmp/blackwell-ldmatrix-probe.ll (4,153 bytes; SHA-256 1fac276e40ad836eb70cc7b8001adbf31fb29e1ef3791c95917cf878b2b8d211). Literal _p64 and _p32 remain part of the LLVM symbol before the .p3 suffix. sm_101a at PTX 8.6 and sm_101f at PTX 8.8 emit successfully, but both abort instruction selection at PTX 9.0; the minimum-only catalog therefore excludes both non-monotonic spellings.

SASS contains HFMA2.MMA.RELU and the two HMNMX2 predicate forms for min/max. PTX 6.5 rejected all three as requiring 7.0; sm_75 rejected all three as requiring sm_80. Tool `/usr/local/cuda-13.3/bin/ptxas` reports `CUDA 13.3 V13.3.33 build cuda_13.3.r13.3/compiler.37862127_0` (SHA-256 `7fdd01a4cf50e30746da98989c9272a907f491e6fd7fecfda14642e4375f88fb`).

A compute_75 LTOIR negative reached nvJitLink but failed terminal PTX compilation because all three forms require sm_80.   - `llvm_nvptx` uses `typed_nvvm` from profile `rust-llvm-22.1.2-1cb4e383` at PTX 7.0 / sm_80+: status `validated` (`LLVM version 22.1.2-rust-1.96.0-nightly`, SHA-256 `ff1dcdc18a5441003d43c5df267b43e2b6dd3efccca38c20bb2801885626331e`)
    - declaration canonicalization on `sm_80, ptx70`: `succeeded` — LLVM canonicalized the shared-pointer declaration with convergent and nocallback attributes.
    - backend codegen on `sm_80, ptx70`: `succeeded` — The selected Rust LLVM backend emitted exact mbarrier.arrive.shared.b64 PTX.
    - PTX assembly on `sm_80, ptx70`: `succeeded` — CUDA 13.3 ptxas accepted the emitted PTX for sm_80. Tool `/usr/local/cuda-13.3/bin/ptxas` reports `CUDA 13.3 V13.3.33 build cuda_13.3.r13.3/compiler.37862127_0` (SHA-256 `7fdd01a4cf50e30746da98989c9272a907f491e6fd7fecfda14642e4375f88fb`).
  - `lib_nvvm` uses `inline_ptx` from profile `cuda-13.3-libnvvm-13.3.33` at PTX 7.0 / sm_80+: status `validated` (`CUDA toolkit 13.3; nvcc/libNVVM release V13.3.33`, SHA-256 `b6a5db233133fb0ed693f8cb8aeb1d405254ce0ab10364478985292ec98cfcf0`)
    - backend codegen on `sm_80, ptx93`: `succeeded` — CUDA 13.3 libNVVM compiled the production barrier example to 4,616-byte LTOIR (SHA-256 63f7ec756a55d4fc813edda5424f2ec599969a2f96bca81c4260414a5f20049c).
    - device link on `sm_80, ptx93`: `succeeded` — CUDA 13.3 nvJitLink produced a 6,816-byte sm_80 cubin (SHA-256 7fc77da879df04e4ab109d1a74e5504ccb3e78afb2518eb6b1b74e06fb1dbcb5); cuobjdump and an isolated attribution kernel showed mbarrier.arrive as MEMBAR.ALL.CTA plus ATOMS.ARRIVE.64. Tool `/usr/local/cuda-13.3/lib64/libnvJitLink.so.13.3.33` reports `V13.3.33` (SHA-256 `3ba1e744347cd68617b862eccfd98b125482e882b7a6319f42abc9a768513db8`). Artifact `cubin`.

Tool `/usr/local/cuda-13.3/targets/x86_64-linux/lib/libnvJitLink.so.13.3.33` reports `V13.3.33` (SHA-256 `3ba1e744347cd68617b862eccfd98b125482e882b7a6319f42abc9a768513db8`). Artifact `cubin`.
- `min_f16x2`: runtime `not recorded`
  - `llvm_nvptx` uses `inline_ptx` from profile `rust-llvm-22.1.2-1cb4e383` at PTX 7.0 / sm_80+: status `validated` (`LLVM version 22.1.2-rust-1.96.0-nightly`, SHA-256 `ff1dcdc18a5441003d43c5df267b43e2b6dd3efccca38c20bb2801885626331e`)
    - backend codegen on `sm_80, ptx70`: `succeeded` — The selected Rust LLVM 22.1.2 backend retained the exact pure inline-PTX instruction in the combined PTX 7.0/sm_80 probe. Independent typed calls to the exact LLVM NVVM symbols emitted the same fma.rn.relu.f16x2, min.f16x2, and max.f16x2 instructions; sm_75 failed exact upper-group selection.
    - PTX assembly on `sm_80, ptx70`: `succeeded` — CUDA 13.3 ptxas accepted the 1,163-byte PTX 7.0 module at sm_80 and produced a 3,496-byte cubin (SHA-256 5fa2890550ffe3793eadd967cc05c25b1bd9541c9a1404ef1ca2a67e7a9856f1). SASS contains HFMA2.MMA.RELU and the two HMNMX2 predicate forms for min/max. PTX 6.5 rejected all three as requiring 7.0; sm_75 rejected all three as requiring sm_80. Tool `/usr/local/cuda-13.3/bin/ptxas` reports `CUDA 13.3 V13.3.33 build cuda_13.3.r13.3/compiler.37862127_0` (SHA-256 `7fdd01a4cf50e30746da98989c9272a907f491e6fd7fecfda14642e4375f88fb`).
  - `lib_nvvm` uses `inline_ptx` from profile `cuda-13.3-libnvvm-13.3.33` at PTX 7.0 / sm_80+: status `validated` (`CUDA toolkit 13.3; nvcc/libNVVM release V13.3.33`, SHA-256 `b6a5db233133fb0ed693f8cb8aeb1d405254ce0ab10364478985292ec98cfcf0`)
    - backend codegen on `sm_80, ptx93`: `succeeded` — CUDA 13.3 libNVVM accepted the exact route at compute_80 as part of the combined three-form upper probe and produced 1,860-byte LTOIR (SHA-256 54983753f6c883e98ddef424042a704c0e38f32fcff6620ef12cf4734543d549). The instruction floor is PTX 7.0/sm_80.
    - device link on `sm_80, ptx93`: `succeeded` — nvJitLink produced a 3,496-byte sm_80 cubin (SHA-256 f70d50b154c66b2dc59909f29f449274a86d8300048982b8b1aa17b276193fe0). cuobjdump SASS contains HFMA2.MMA.RELU and both HMNMX2 predicate forms, matching the LLVM/ptxas path. A compute_75 LTOIR negative reached nvJitLink but failed terminal PTX compilation because all three forms require sm_80. Tool `/usr/local/cuda-13.3/lib64/libnvJitLink.so.13.3.33` reports `V13.3.33` (SHA-256 `3ba1e744347cd68617b862eccfd98b125482e882b7a6319f42abc9a768513db8`). Artifact `cubin`.

    - backend codegen on `compute_89, ptx93`: `succeeded` — libNVVM compiled a 13717-byte typed-pointer IR fixture (SHA-256 add55fcb64ff5a318dc20e775f57cd17d775dac1215a43828a3b2d1b6b513cd6) to 9034-byte PTX (SHA-256 902cb73837cc693df499fd107cc437dd344315e57e4b29ad8b980d269452f0dc) and 6932-byte LTOIR (SHA-256 ff6619b9b8cf44f6bec07aeca7d6f2ed5bffda01501d0d0a96a06bf4fdd48f2b), with all 16 forms live. An eight-call convergent duplicate fixture remained eight instructions. The same fixture emitted all forms for compute_90 and also emitted compute_87 intermediates that terminal tools rejected.
    - PTX assembly on `sm_89, ptx93`: `succeeded` — ptxas produced a 10088-byte cubin (SHA-256 bb4610329a1066be83c4306c9b46b5f58300387b496a33415420537be89e4cbc); its 43177-byte SASS dump (SHA-256 2693f93687940907f276c0383cbdb32e17a2b4d7a94886cc14895fea9e5d2f6b) contains 16 QMMA instructions. The same all-form PTX assembled for newer sm_90 into a 13296-byte cubin. Tool `/usr/local/cuda-13.3/bin/ptxas` reports `CUDA 13.3 V13.3.33 build cuda_13.3.r13.3/compiler.37862127_0` (SHA-256 `7fdd01a4cf50e30746da98989c9272a907f491e6fd7fecfda14642e4375f88fb`). Artifact `cubin`.
    - device link on `sm_89, ptx93`: `succeeded` — nvJitLink produced a 10088-byte cubin (SHA-256 bd21c8f939a126734119f9b6e0cfbf45d5552ceb6069c5f8de6f4209234b8640); its SASS is byte-identical to the direct ptxas dump and contains 16 QMMA instructions. Newer sm_90 device linking also succeeded with cubin SHA-256 a83dcd59c27fad87a17e5308e245639743bdddc5e7327aa01acff9d624c27c99. Tool `/usr/local/cuda-13.3/lib64/libnvJitLink.so.13.3.33` reports `V13.3.33` (SHA-256 `3ba1e744347cd68617b862eccfd98b125482e882b7a6319f42abc9a768513db8`). Artifact `cubin`.
    - PTX assembly on `sm_87, ptx93`: `failed` — ptxas rejected all forms because standard FP8 MMA requires sm_89 or higher; the diagnostic SHA-256 is d25b354f14d618d6202a98bbfe3292fb19e1a0894aea8f5aba28d0e2530a071f. Tool `/usr/local/cuda-13.3/bin/ptxas` reports `CUDA 13.3 V13.3.33 build cuda_13.3.r13.3/compiler.37862127_0` (SHA-256 `7fdd01a4cf50e30746da98989c9272a907f491e6fd7fecfda14642e4375f88fb`).
    - device link on `sm_87, ptx93`: `failed` — nvJitLink rejected the compute_87 LTOIR because standard FP8 MMA requires sm_89 or higher; the diagnostic SHA-256 is bf2ed0ca6d60259bc52c0449bbbf7a68af6811b581e338d04fdeda562b6587fc. Tool `/usr/local/cuda-13.3/lib64/libnvJitLink.so.13.3.33` reports `V13.3.33` (SHA-256 `3ba1e744347cd68617b862eccfd98b125482e882b7a6319f42abc9a768513db8`).

  - `llvm_nvptx` uses `inline_ptx` from profile `rust-llvm-22.1.2-1cb4e383` at PTX 8.7 / sm_89+: status `validated` (`LLVM version 22.1.2-rust-1.96.0-nightly`, SHA-256 `ff1dcdc18a5441003d43c5df267b43e2b6dd3efccca38c20bb2801885626331e`)
    - declaration canonicalization on `sm_89, ptx87`: `succeeded` — LLVM verified all 16 declarations and canonicalized them to nocallback nounwind memory(none), without convergent. The 14810-byte source and 15723-byte canonical IR have SHA-256 06c1e45eb81825111c318a3a168a39c51ec2367ec298170aa33c68913c79e18c and 2d6806628e2e041fbd723990a0bcca1de0a95b688984fcefe937e3e602f2fc7d. O2 merged eight representative typed calls to four, while the convergent sideeffect inline fixture retained all eight calls. The typed CSE source/output hashes are 8875c8c486b6e07e1c29ad6e3f050a42b49d7d670ea16d36b1cc24b2e9961500 and 5dadcecbdff3a4fcc2d1eed72ca2b066c8b541aedaad69df839a5c00ce38137e; inline hashes are afe744162d4cfb8d06393b39d65d16354d5af131e3e75f13a8891a361be6e23c and 4574a453fe7ef2d8d44d2cbe7973a02a31bb40459a2461f22c924d86731a13e8.
    - backend codegen on `sm_89, ptx87`: `succeeded` — Pinned LLVM emitted all 16 typed forms in 8609-byte PTX (SHA-256 69d1ed4f97813f2410219952455bd8f0d17930290be1f129edf88c0e270f7506) and all 16 convergent inline forms in 9234-byte PTX (SHA-256 46bdcf6be3a1c0811baca29a4ac3c3b421216c03b33376ffb9fa97e304479329).
    - PTX assembly on `sm_89, ptx87`: `succeeded` — ptxas accepted all four k16/F16 forms at PTX 8.7. The PTX and 3496-byte cubin SHA-256 values are bb964ef8b6262d4c55d102c8660ffde741c833c50669fda6040edb754792876a and 0ec074dc7c5ed05604bebdd41e483f8a636c32bb90d8e6683cc74e7a163a9e63. The aggregate 16-form fixture also assembled at newer sm_90/PTX 8.7. Tool `/usr/local/cuda-13.3/bin/ptxas` reports `CUDA 13.3 V13.3.33 build cuda_13.3.r13.3/compiler.37862127_0` (SHA-256 `7fdd01a4cf50e30746da98989c9272a907f491e6fd7fecfda14642e4375f88fb`).     - PTX assembly on `sm_89, ptx86`: `failed` — ptxas rejected every k16 form below PTX 8.7; the diagnostic SHA-256 is 36ca3350917752b188d197cf2e28d18d183be3dd01c12ea3847e9c5bb82f15af.     - PTX assembly on `sm_87, ptx87`: `failed` — LLVM's typed selector and CUDA ptxas both rejected the family below sm_89.     - device link on `sm_89, ptx93`: `succeeded` — nvJitLink produced a 10088-byte cubin (SHA-256 bd21c8f939a126734119f9b6e0cfbf45d5552ceb6069c5f8de6f4209234b8640); its SASS is byte-identical to the direct ptxas dump and contains 16 QMMA instructions. Newer sm_90 device linking also succeeded with cubin SHA-256 a83dcd59c27fad87a17e5308e245639743bdddc5e7327aa01acff9d624c27c99. Tool `/usr/local/cuda-13.3/lib64/libnvJitLink.so.13.3.33` reports `V13.3.33` (SHA-256 `3ba1e744347cd68617b862eccfd98b125482e882b7a6319f42abc9a768513db8`). Artifact `cubin`.
    - PTX assembly on `sm_87, ptx93`: `failed` — ptxas rejected all forms because standard FP8 MMA requires sm_89 or higher; the diagnostic SHA-256 is d25b354f14d618d6202a98bbfe3292fb19e1a0894aea8f5aba28d0e2530a071f. Tool `/usr/local/cuda-13.3/bin/ptxas` reports `CUDA 13.3 V13.3.33 build cuda_13.3.r13.3/compiler.37862127_0` (SHA-256 `7fdd01a4cf50e30746da98989c9272a907f491e6fd7fecfda14642e4375f88fb`).
    - device link on `sm_87, ptx93`: `failed` — nvJitLink rejected the compute_87 LTOIR because standard FP8 MMA requires sm_89 or higher; the diagnostic SHA-256 is bf2ed0ca6d60259bc52c0449bbbf7a68af6811b581e338d04fdeda562b6587fc. Tool `/usr/local/cuda-13.3/lib64/libnvJitLink.so.13.3.33` reports `V13.3.33` (SHA-256 `3ba1e744347cd68617b862eccfd98b125482e882b7a6319f42abc9a768513db8`).

  - `llvm_nvptx` uses `inline_ptx` from profile `rust-llvm-22.1.2-1cb4e383` at PTX 7.0 / sm_80+: status `validated` (`LLVM version 22.1.2-rust-1.96.0-nightly`, SHA-256 `ff1dcdc18a5441003d43c5df267b43e2b6dd3efccca38c20bb2801885626331e`)
    - PTX assembly on `sm_75, ptx70`: `failed` — ptxas rejected the form below sm_80; the diagnostic SHA-256 is 73ef62d63bacc273dbfc152d2cb3acb93bfe3c744b3003e49cf66cd8a47e0b5d.
    - declaration canonicalization on `sm_80, ptx70`: `succeeded` — LLVM canonicalized the five declarations to nocallback nounwind memory(none) without convergent. The 6254-byte source and 6413-byte canonical IR have SHA-256 883f351fb1d6eebd787e1312c2365c2dd5ff5a4ad852a6a8ee71901b3dc2ee16 and 20c9a527d322dea1f871debfa500120cccdb8ab1174fa73403f6bfc0f57a1992. O2 merged ten typed calls to five, while ten convergent side-effecting inline calls remained ten; the typed source/output hashes are 139d3203e4622002bf662064a1e83e7c1b4a8c57a82854bfd24fb7a7831d2e88 and cd57bd9a4f2204f7bd69b71fde7e709e183d7815e9e708c786d9c3dd7e087ac8, and the inline hashes are 21548f313f29c146ea089c082ccf5903ac17777dddd7bd663dbd9b59ea53720c and 040d2cc2dc6029aa767212a4b44cfd430c557ee81c2e76bb5ffa740a0a3cd691.
    - backend codegen on `sm_80, ptx70`: `succeeded` — Pinned LLVM emitted each exact MMA form once in 7366-byte PTX with SHA-256 4da1521d328388d9b779f0f070db4d51fe65127288f62a1d69c09834d444f610. The typed comparison fixture also emitted all five forms, but is not selected because it lacks convergence.

- `mma_sp_m16n8k128_s32_s4`: runtime `unexecuted`
  - `llvm_nvptx` uses `inline_ptx` from profile `rust-llvm-22.1.2-1cb4e383` at PTX 7.1 / sm_80+: status `validated` (`LLVM version 22.1.2-rust-1.96.0-nightly`, SHA-256 `ff1dcdc18a5441003d43c5df267b43e2b6dd3efccca38c20bb2801885626331e`)
    - backend codegen on `sm_80, ptx71`: `succeeded` — The 4,867-byte combined public-order inline-PTX fixture (SHA-256 98ef7b8e03f9f690c118f360514f74e6da85151fd83b1be71c8b102083abb76e) covered all eight m16n8k128 variants with selector 0. Pinned LLVM emitted 3,913-byte PTX (SHA-256 0f39eba80daa327cf5276f2942e940c5f2df3cab586db75af10210b21fffdfd6) containing exactly eight standard m16n8k128 instructions. An auxiliary 4,528-byte typed LLVM fixture (SHA-256 3d5971c2a14df66be17427c8da533c2f29745173ff867b366eeac739495959c5) used A4, B4, C4, metadata, selector source order and emitted 13,602-byte PTX (SHA-256 601e40fc487232c248466c5a0cc688cb85cee2cac45e2a571991438dfa78aa23) with the same eight forms. The imported record has fourteen i32 arguments, four i32 results, ImmArg<arg13>, IntrNoCallback, IntrNoMem, and Range<arg13,0,1>. Pinned LLVM rejected selector 1 (580-byte fixture SHA-256 a810dcb7589210cd2ea8d46d261c0841dc4207f984c283bb4f7f7ebe29c68665) and a dynamic selector (603-byte fixture SHA-256 5573f9ea9fd0f07669c5f54af9501332933b66a4ff7bfbcc103f0f4cf2b498d4). Every standard-metadata nibble must encode two distinct 2-bit indices; 0x0, 0x5, 0xa, and 0xf are undefined behavior. This record retained exact wrapping s4-by-s4 PTX.
    - PTX assembly on `sm_80, ptx71`: `succeeded` — CUDA 13.3 ptxas accepted selector 0 at PTX 7.1/sm_80 (258-byte fixture SHA-256 308e16eaf954fa79243181ba7e91c28f5310e1a5b275e80092bd87458c2d48b8), issued only the ordered-metadata performance advisory, and assembled the full LLVM output to a 4,008-byte cubin (SHA-256 50f8efe377dee2b7502120e012557548b6af95f53fcb395ae1d34060d0169b9a) retaining exactly eight IMMA.SP.168128 instructions. It rejected selector 1 (SHA-256 7bfbafd9e5a67d3de2bd4e0bf55b4be6865569c374bdc64962e184385b353679), a register selector (SHA-256 93909da87dbe1a1b12a7064b4b0b4103c710bec837ab605326091e8888b10bb5), PTX 7.0 (SHA-256 b3a44520c93f1500442c99d3eeaae69e826fd28c3afd6f7f9345d3ce2a26d37f), and sm_75 (SHA-256 46c14a11d7814b7a83f082246218f5c8c5107844cd494958191cb9bd0019d6e9). Tool `/usr/local/cuda-13.3/bin/ptxas` reports `CUDA 13.3 V13.3.33 build cuda_13.3.r13.3/compiler.37862127_0` (SHA-256 `7fdd01a4cf50e30746da98989c9272a907f491e6fd7fecfda14642e4375f88fb`). Artifact `cubin`.
  - `lib_nvvm` uses `inline_ptx` from profile `cuda-13.3-libnvvm-13.3.33` at PTX 7.1 / sm_80+: status `validated` (`CUDA toolkit 13.3; nvcc/libNVVM release V13.3.33`, SHA-256 `b6a5db233133fb0ed693f8cb8aeb1d405254ce0ab10364478985292ec98cfcf0`)
    - backend codegen on `compute_80, ptx93`: `succeeded` — The same 4,867-byte NVVM IR fixture (SHA-256 98ef7b8e03f9f690c118f360514f74e6da85151fd83b1be71c8b102083abb76e) passed CUDA 13.3 libNVVM and produced 3,811-byte NUL-terminated PTX (SHA-256 595ec2f639ccb02e83580254efa78758160ccb8148198a08b932b9f7e2962afa) containing all eight variants with selector 0. Every standard-metadata nibble must encode two distinct 2-bit indices; 0x0, 0x5, 0xa, and 0xf are undefined behavior. This record retained exact wrapping s4-by-s4 PTX. Production cargo oxide emit-ltoir generated_intrinsics --arch=sm_80 emitted 374,720-byte NVVM IR (SHA-256 d8180177c4f4e54c641636f7d894fa01314ca6c9251a423ef9027e1985ae120b) and 104,032-byte LTOIR (SHA-256 9f451bb1d66eb39596d0639eb95cf2712e82975f518cf89829eaf18fb5fb15f0). Its 25-byte sm_80 target manifest (SHA-256 06ecfb1b0100fd9886f26871a9fc48350b612a35370b154b07d83a3a57fbbadb) and 49-byte compile-options file (SHA-256 069d630cf5359ec49a571d9074d106aeda207bc279693d2a9ce3c21b168ad03a) pin the production inputs.
    - PTX assembly on `sm_80, ptx93`: `succeeded` — After removing libNVVM's single trailing NUL, CUDA 13.3 ptxas accepted the 3,810-byte PTX (SHA-256 302d6da269cd36de7991678afcc1b0c13be08302e4338bf9a170831d0e7f4dea), issued only the ordered-metadata performance advisory, and produced a 4,008-byte cubin (SHA-256 dca42ca41d8127353ab0a9a2aa1673bc9d5a1deff86d0c0aed18b7b5c88c4572) with exactly eight IMMA.SP.168128 instructions. The PTX 7.0, sm_75, selector 1, and dynamic-selector negatives are recorded by the paired pinned-LLVM evidence. Tool `/usr/local/cuda-13.3/bin/ptxas` reports `CUDA 13.3 V13.3.33 build cuda_13.3.r13.3/compiler.37862127_0` (SHA-256 `7fdd01a4cf50e30746da98989c9272a907f491e6fd7fecfda14642e4375f88fb`). Artifact `cubin`.
    - device link on `sm_80, ptx93`: `succeeded` — The direct CUDA 13.3 libNVVM-to-nvJitLink route produced a 4,008-byte cubin with SHA-256 e304b99f1ddaddb561a7cf45c39fa3b97c5252091ffafbcca7577dfb60d395fc retaining exactly eight IMMA.SP.168128 instructions. Production linking of that LTOIR through the repository nvJitLink helper produced a 30,952-byte sm_80 cubin (SHA-256 9c9f270e43b9732e814f899d9aeba2dc6f20aef85769bc83eaae8e345cb82ae6). Independent 92,603-byte nvdisasm (SHA-256 dd862d50b376cc5ae8cd083b71d5da500a751b44c4016ab5d6b3c838960cbc7e) and 349,080-byte cuobjdump (SHA-256 44f234c740a4c6f1b3dd917b979a9f495e7e838343130185febc9c604a3e84ca) views each retained exactly 80 IMMA.SP instructions: 20 m16n8k32, 40 m16n8k64, and 20 m16n8k128. The 16 raw and four compatibility calls added by this batch account for 20 instructions. The coverage kernel is unlaunched, so runtime validation remains unexecuted. Tool `/usr/local/cuda-13.3/lib64/libnvJitLink.so.13.3.33` reports `V13.3.33` (SHA-256 `3ba1e744347cd68617b862eccfd98b125482e882b7a6319f42abc9a768513db8`). Artifact `cubin`.

    - device link on `sm_80, ptx93`: `succeeded` — The direct CUDA 13.3 libNVVM-to-nvJitLink route produced a 4,008-byte cubin with SHA-256 e304b99f1ddaddb561a7cf45c39fa3b97c5252091ffafbcca7577dfb60d395fc retaining exactly eight IMMA.SP.168128 instructions. Production linking of that LTOIR through the repository nvJitLink helper produced a 30,952-byte sm_80 cubin (SHA-256 9c9f270e43b9732e814f899d9aeba2dc6f20aef85769bc83eaae8e345cb82ae6). Independent 92,603-byte nvdisasm (SHA-256 dd862d50b376cc5ae8cd083b71d5da500a751b44c4016ab5d6b3c838960cbc7e) and 349,080-byte cuobjdump (SHA-256 44f234c740a4c6f1b3dd917b979a9f495e7e838343130185febc9c604a3e84ca) views each retained exactly 80 IMMA.SP instructions: 20 m16n8k32, 40 m16n8k64, and 20 m16n8k128. The 16 raw and four compatibility calls added by this batch account for 20 instructions. The coverage kernel is unlaunched, so runtime validation remains unexecuted. Tool `/usr/local/cuda-13.3/lib64/libnvJitLink.so.13.3.33` reports `V13.3.33` (SHA-256 `3ba1e744347cd68617b862eccfd98b125482e882b7a6319f42abc9a768513db8`). Artifact `cubin`.
- `mma_sp_m16n8k128_s32_s4_u4`: runtime `unexecuted`
  - `llvm_nvptx` uses `inline_ptx` from profile `rust-llvm-22.1.2-1cb4e383` at PTX 7.1 / sm_80+: status `validated` (`LLVM version 22.1.2-rust-1.96.0-nightly`, SHA-256 `ff1dcdc18a5441003d43c5df267b43e2b6dd3efccca38c20bb2801885626331e`)
    - backend codegen on `sm_80, ptx71`: `succeeded` — The 4,867-byte combined public-order inline-PTX fixture (SHA-256 98ef7b8e03f9f690c118f360514f74e6da85151fd83b1be71c8b102083abb76e) covered all eight m16n8k128 variants with selector 0. Pinned LLVM emitted 3,913-byte PTX (SHA-256 0f39eba80daa327cf5276f2942e940c5f2df3cab586db75af10210b21fffdfd6) containing exactly eight standard m16n8k128 instructions. An auxiliary 4,528-byte typed LLVM fixture (SHA-256 3d5971c2a14df66be17427c8da533c2f29745173ff867b366eeac739495959c5) used A4, B4, C4, metadata, selector source order and emitted 13,602-byte PTX (SHA-256 601e40fc487232c248466c5a0cc688cb85cee2cac45e2a571991438dfa78aa23) with the same eight forms. The imported record has fourteen i32 arguments, four i32 results, ImmArg<arg13>, IntrNoCallback, IntrNoMem, and Range<arg13,0,1>. Pinned LLVM rejected selector 1 (580-byte fixture SHA-256 a810dcb7589210cd2ea8d46d261c0841dc4207f984c283bb4f7f7ebe29c68665) and a dynamic selector (603-byte fixture SHA-256 5573f9ea9fd0f07669c5f54af9501332933b66a4ff7bfbcc103f0f4cf2b498d4). Every standard-metadata nibble must encode two distinct 2-bit indices; 0x0, 0x5, 0xa, and 0xf are undefined behavior. This record retained exact wrapping s4-by-u4 PTX.
    - PTX assembly on `sm_80, ptx71`: `succeeded` — CUDA 13.3 ptxas accepted selector 0 at PTX 7.1/sm_80 (258-byte fixture SHA-256 308e16eaf954fa79243181ba7e91c28f5310e1a5b275e80092bd87458c2d48b8), issued only the ordered-metadata performance advisory, and assembled the full LLVM output to a 4,008-byte cubin (SHA-256 50f8efe377dee2b7502120e012557548b6af95f53fcb395ae1d34060d0169b9a) retaining exactly eight IMMA.SP.168128 instructions. It rejected selector 1 (SHA-256 7bfbafd9e5a67d3de2bd4e0bf55b4be6865569c374bdc64962e184385b353679), a register selector (SHA-256 93909da87dbe1a1b12a7064b4b0b4103c710bec837ab605326091e8888b10bb5), PTX 7.0 (SHA-256 b3a44520c93f1500442c99d3eeaae69e826fd28c3afd6f7f9345d3ce2a26d37f), and sm_75 (SHA-256 46c14a11d7814b7a83f082246218f5c8c5107844cd494958191cb9bd0019d6e9). Tool `/usr/local/cuda-13.3/bin/ptxas` reports `CUDA 13.3 V13.3.33 build cuda_13.3.r13.3/compiler.37862127_0` (SHA-256 `7fdd01a4cf50e30746da98989c9272a907f491e6fd7fecfda14642e4375f88fb`). Artifact `cubin`.
  - `lib_nvvm` uses `inline_ptx` from profile `cuda-13.3-libnvvm-13.3.33` at PTX 7.1 / sm_80+: status `validated` (`CUDA toolkit 13.3; nvcc/libNVVM release V13.3.33`, SHA-256 `b6a5db233133fb0ed693f8cb8aeb1d405254ce0ab10364478985292ec98cfcf0`)
    - backend codegen on `compute_80, ptx93`: `succeeded` — The same 4,867-byte NVVM IR fixture (SHA-256 98ef7b8e03f9f690c118f360514f74e6da85151fd83b1be71c8b102083abb76e) passed CUDA 13.3 libNVVM and produced 3,811-byte NUL-terminated PTX (SHA-256 595ec2f639ccb02e83580254efa78758160ccb8148198a08b932b9f7e2962afa) containing all eight variants with selector 0. Every standard-metadata nibble must encode two distinct 2-bit indices; 0x0, 0x5, 0xa, and 0xf are undefined behavior. This record retained exact wrapping s4-by-u4 PTX. Production cargo oxide emit-ltoir generated_intrinsics --arch=sm_80 emitted 374,720-byte NVVM IR (SHA-256 d8180177c4f4e54c641636f7d894fa01314ca6c9251a423ef9027e1985ae120b) and 104,032-byte LTOIR (SHA-256 9f451bb1d66eb39596d0639eb95cf2712e82975f518cf89829eaf18fb5fb15f0). Its 25-byte sm_80 target manifest (SHA-256 06ecfb1b0100fd9886f26871a9fc48350b612a35370b154b07d83a3a57fbbadb) and 49-byte compile-options file (SHA-256 069d630cf5359ec49a571d9074d106aeda207bc279693d2a9ce3c21b168ad03a) pin the production inputs.
    - PTX assembly on `sm_80, ptx93`: `succeeded` — After removing libNVVM's single trailing NUL, CUDA 13.3 ptxas accepted the 3,810-byte PTX (SHA-256 302d6da269cd36de7991678afcc1b0c13be08302e4338bf9a170831d0e7f4dea), issued only the ordered-metadata performance advisory, and produced a 4,008-byte cubin (SHA-256 dca42ca41d8127353ab0a9a2aa1673bc9d5a1deff86d0c0aed18b7b5c88c4572) with exactly eight IMMA.SP.168128 instructions. The PTX 7.0, sm_75, selector 1, and dynamic-selector negatives are recorded by the paired pinned-LLVM evidence. Artifact `cubin`.

An auxiliary 4,528-byte typed LLVM fixture (SHA-256 3d5971c2a14df66be17427c8da533c2f29745173ff867b366eeac739495959c5) used A4, B4, C4, metadata, selector source order and emitted 13,602-byte PTX (SHA-256 601e40fc487232c248466c5a0cc688cb85cee2cac45e2a571991438dfa78aa23) with the same eight forms. The imported record has fourteen i32 arguments, four i32 results, ImmArg<arg13>, IntrNoCallback, IntrNoMem, and Range<arg13,0,1>. Pinned LLVM rejected selector 1 (580-byte fixture SHA-256 a810dcb7589210cd2ea8d46d261c0841dc4207f984c283bb4f7f7ebe29c68665) and a dynamic selector (603-byte fixture SHA-256 5573f9ea9fd0f07669c5f54af9501332933b66a4ff7bfbcc103f0f4cf2b498d4). Every standard-metadata nibble must encode two distinct 2-bit indices; 0x0, 0x5, 0xa, and 0xf are undefined behavior. This record retained exact wrapping u4-by-s4 PTX.
    - PTX assembly on `sm_80, ptx71`: `succeeded` — CUDA 13.3 ptxas accepted selector 0 at PTX 7.1/sm_80 (258-byte fixture SHA-256 308e16eaf954fa79243181ba7e91c28f5310e1a5b275e80092bd87458c2d48b8), issued only the ordered-metadata performance advisory, and assembled the full LLVM output to a 4,008-byte cubin (SHA-256 50f8efe377dee2b7502120e012557548b6af95f53fcb395ae1d34060d0169b9a) retaining exactly eight IMMA.SP.168128 instructions. It rejected selector 1 (SHA-256 7bfbafd9e5a67d3de2bd4e0bf55b4be6865569c374bdc64962e184385b353679), a register selector (SHA-256 93909da87dbe1a1b12a7064b4b0b4103c710bec837ab605326091e8888b10bb5), PTX 7.0 (SHA-256 b3a44520c93f1500442c99d3eeaae69e826fd28c3afd6f7f9345d3ce2a26d37f), and sm_75 (SHA-256 46c14a11d7814b7a83f082246218f5c8c5107844cd494958191cb9bd0019d6e9). Tool `/usr/local/cuda-13.3/bin/ptxas` reports `CUDA 13.3 V13.3.33 build cuda_13.3.r13.3/compiler.37862127_0` (SHA-256 `7fdd01a4cf50e30746da98989c9272a907f491e6fd7fecfda14642e4375f88fb`). Artifact `cubin`.
  - `lib_nvvm` uses `inline_ptx` from profile `cuda-13.3-libnvvm-13.3.33` at PTX 7.1 / sm_80+: status `validated` (`CUDA toolkit 13.3; nvcc/libNVVM release V13.3.33`, SHA-256 `b6a5db233133fb0ed693f8cb8aeb1d405254ce0ab10364478985292ec98cfcf0`)
    - backend codegen on `compute_80, ptx93`: `succeeded` — The same 4,867-byte NVVM IR fixture (SHA-256 98ef7b8e03f9f690c118f360514f74e6da85151fd83b1be71c8b102083abb76e) passed CUDA 13.3 libNVVM and produced 3,811-byte NUL-terminated PTX (SHA-256 595ec2f639ccb02e83580254efa78758160ccb8148198a08b932b9f7e2962afa) containing all eight variants with selector 0. Every standard-metadata nibble must encode two distinct 2-bit indices; 0x0, 0x5, 0xa, and 0xf are undefined behavior. This record retained exact wrapping u4-by-s4 PTX. Production cargo oxide emit-ltoir generated_intrinsics --arch=sm_80 emitted 374,720-byte NVVM IR (SHA-256 d8180177c4f4e54c641636f7d894fa01314ca6c9251a423ef9027e1985ae120b) and 104,032-byte LTOIR (SHA-256 9f451bb1d66eb39596d0639eb95cf2712e82975f518cf89829eaf18fb5fb15f0). Its 25-byte sm_80 target manifest (SHA-256 06ecfb1b0100fd9886f26871a9fc48350b612a35370b154b07d83a3a57fbbadb) and 49-byte compile-options file (SHA-256 069d630cf5359ec49a571d9074d106aeda207bc279693d2a9ce3c21b168ad03a) pin the production inputs.
    - PTX assembly on `sm_80, ptx93`: `succeeded` — After removing libNVVM's single trailing NUL, CUDA 13.3 ptxas accepted the 3,810-byte PTX (SHA-256 302d6da269cd36de7991678afcc1b0c13be08302e4338bf9a170831d0e7f4dea), issued only the ordered-metadata performance advisory, and produced a 4,008-byte cubin (SHA-256 dca42ca41d8127353ab0a9a2aa1673bc9d5a1deff86d0c0aed18b7b5c88c4572) with exactly eight IMMA.SP.168128 instructions. The PTX 7.0, sm_75, selector 1, and dynamic-selector negatives are recorded by the paired pinned-LLVM evidence. Tool `/usr/local/cuda-13.3/bin/ptxas` reports `CUDA 13.3 V13.3.33 build cuda_13.3.r13.3/compiler.37862127_0` (SHA-256 `7fdd01a4cf50e30746da98989c9272a907f491e6fd7fecfda14642e4375f88fb`). Artifact `cubin`.
    - device link on `sm_80, ptx93`: `succeeded` — The direct CUDA 13.3 libNVVM-to-nvJitLink route produced a 4,008-byte cubin with SHA-256 e304b99f1ddaddb561a7cf45c39fa3b97c5252091ffafbcca7577dfb60d395fc retaining exactly eight IMMA.SP.168128 instructions. Production linking of that LTOIR through the repository nvJitLink helper produced a 30,952-byte sm_80 cubin (SHA-256 9c9f270e43b9732e814f899d9aeba2dc6f20aef85769bc83eaae8e345cb82ae6). Independent 92,603-byte nvdisasm (SHA-256 dd862d50b376cc5ae8cd083b71d5da500a751b44c4016ab5d6b3c838960cbc7e) and 349,080-byte cuobjdump (SHA-256 44f234c740a4c6f1b3dd917b979a9f495e7e838343130185febc9c604a3e84ca) views each retained exactly 80 IMMA.SP instructions: 20 m16n8k32, 40 m16n8k64, and 20 m16n8k128. The 16 raw and four compatibility calls added by this batch account for 20 instructions. The coverage kernel is unlaunched, so runtime validation remains unexecuted. Tool `/usr/local/cuda-13.3/lib64/libnvJitLink.so.13.3.33` reports `V13.3.33` (SHA-256 `3ba1e744347cd68617b862eccfd98b125482e882b7a6319f42abc9a768513db8`). Artifact `cubin`.
