- `mma_sp_m16n8k64_s32_s4`: runtime `unexecuted`
  - `llvm_nvptx` uses `inline_ptx` from profile `rust-llvm-22.1.2-1cb4e383` at PTX 7.1 / sm_80+: status `validated` (`LLVM version 22.1.2-rust-1.96.0-nightly`, SHA-256 `ff1dcdc18a5441003d43c5df267b43e2b6dd3efccca38c20bb2801885626331e`)
    - backend codegen on `sm_80, ptx71`: `succeeded` — The 7,822-byte combined public-order inline-PTX fixture (SHA-256 ea46feacc7fcd1c5ae768178ec7ad5cb97a85c892ca6317fbe4a13c72dafbc33) covered all eight m16n8k64 variants with selectors 0 and 1. Pinned LLVM emitted 5,081-byte PTX (SHA-256 d1c8848ea43659574ab6881b36791fa812f2c672b010e37cf735b83a999cd0e8) containing exactly 16 standard m16n8k64 instructions. An auxiliary 6,540-byte typed LLVM fixture (SHA-256 9e11fe0ab804292311e9d13d90a5acb1e8478b044b4110868f7ae5774e762e21) used A2, B2, C4, metadata, selector source order and emitted 22,042-byte PTX (SHA-256 837238a344ec6c789a2c63b970466e299da430cebebcd8dda7b6ab56426c633c) with the same 16 forms. The imported record has ten i32 arguments, four i32 results, ImmArg<arg9>, IntrNoCallback, IntrNoMem, and Range<arg9,0,2>. Pinned LLVM rejected selector 2 (487-byte fixture SHA-256 964fe7dd3cd81b441fd4d38cccac420cfea6d219a27208edf408171f26a97c81) and a dynamic selector (510-byte fixture SHA-256 fc193d79e8e3cfc487cd1aa3b4c312c08efc8d5c34998c3221eba7f423480908). Every standard-metadata nibble must encode two distinct 2-bit indices; 0x0, 0x5, 0xa, and 0xf are undefined behavior. This record retained exact wrapping s4-by-s4 PTX.
    - PTX assembly on `sm_80, ptx71`: `succeeded` — CUDA 13.3 ptxas accepted selectors 0 and 1 at PTX 7.1/sm_80 (233-byte fixtures SHA-256 2fdee0d588d2b4f587e59da2ad4e5778a19c69e887017ac331d9cd359b19c794 and 197f1324281ada10b16a5dbd4f65c13808109180b50d29cc5f4524dd849017bb), issued only the ordered-metadata performance advisory, and assembled the full LLVM output to a 4,008-byte cubin (SHA-256 512144584ed413fe89362c698e35b2b128fd144f02b4fa70a26161ae314f203a) retaining exactly 16 IMMA.SP.16864 instructions. It rejected selector 2 (SHA-256 c2d7a5528fa41ef8d04c8e900da46bc41d5cc73e1764c0d10c60aad2e2b6730e), a register selector (SHA-256 d4f972b7c70e68d2b62c86aa181d2eecc73f3bf10e89c570bc478db51218bcb5), PTX 7.0 (SHA-256 d88934934d5bf9fbda0910bd70eadd600e98a4b31bba50e090361dd821bbc90b), and sm_75 (SHA-256 19bf3cb2fa6b19a1e7c1c4cc62c6195b3c8360ebba5785cbc89d46377650c1fa). Tool `/usr/local/cuda-13.3/bin/ptxas` reports `CUDA 13.3 V13.3.33 build cuda_13.3.r13.3/compiler.37862127_0` (SHA-256 `7fdd01a4cf50e30746da98989c9272a907f491e6fd7fecfda14642e4375f88fb`). Artifact `cubin`.
  - `lib_nvvm` uses `inline_ptx` from profile `cuda-13.3-libnvvm-13.3.33` at PTX 7.1 / sm_80+: status `validated` (`CUDA toolkit 13.3; nvcc/libNVVM release V13.3.33`, SHA-256 `b6a5db233133fb0ed693f8cb8aeb1d405254ce0ab10364478985292ec98cfcf0`)
    - backend codegen on `compute_80, ptx93`: `succeeded` — The same 7,822-byte NVVM IR fixture (SHA-256 ea46feacc7fcd1c5ae768178ec7ad5cb97a85c892ca6317fbe4a13c72dafbc33) passed CUDA 13.3 libNVVM and produced 4,979-byte NUL-terminated PTX (SHA-256 abe6ae9238f6137de91c9c2214fc9d7aa78da2bdd6d60a75013f45866c60c38f) containing all eight variants with selectors 0 and 1. Every standard-metadata nibble must encode two distinct 2-bit indices; 0x0, 0x5, 0xa, and 0xf are undefined behavior. This record retained exact wrapping s4-by-s4 PTX. Production cargo oxide emit-ltoir generated_intrinsics --arch=sm_80 emitted 374,720-byte NVVM IR (SHA-256 d8180177c4f4e54c641636f7d894fa01314ca6c9251a423ef9027e1985ae120b) and 104,032-byte LTOIR (SHA-256 9f451bb1d66eb39596d0639eb95cf2712e82975f518cf89829eaf18fb5fb15f0). Its 25-byte sm_80 target manifest (SHA-256 06ecfb1b0100fd9886f26871a9fc48350b612a35370b154b07d83a3a57fbbadb) and 49-byte compile-options file (SHA-256 069d630cf5359ec49a571d9074d106aeda207bc279693d2a9ce3c21b168ad03a) pin the production inputs.
    - PTX assembly on `sm_80, ptx93`: `succeeded` — After removing libNVVM's single trailing NUL, CUDA 13.3 ptxas accepted the 4,978-byte PTX (SHA-256 7b24a91e649c24cd57c20d6923a0940730b9465107178b227921b8d5f0a478d2), issued only the ordered-metadata performance advisory, and produced a 4,008-byte cubin (SHA-256 109d906a6082234019e136181d47d7306c65edbedf5c47e3c46ef56635fa6ca6) with exactly 16 IMMA.SP.16864 instructions. The PTX 7.0, sm_75, selector 2, and dynamic-selector negatives are recorded by the paired pinned-LLVM evidence. Tool `/usr/local/cuda-13.3/bin/ptxas` reports `CUDA 13.3 V13.3.33 build cuda_13.3.r13.3/compiler.37862127_0` (SHA-256 `7fdd01a4cf50e30746da98989c9272a907f491e6fd7fecfda14642e4375f88fb`). Artifact `cubin`.
    - device link on `sm_80, ptx93`: `succeeded` — The direct CUDA 13.3 libNVVM-to-nvJitLink route produced a 4,008-byte cubin with SHA-256 3cc463ee7fdde107bbd51039c7a2259f62196923d049c78703e3662cccc378f5 retaining exactly 16 IMMA.SP.16864 instructions. Production linking of that LTOIR through the repository nvJitLink helper produced a 30,952-byte sm_80 cubin (SHA-256 9c9f270e43b9732e814f899d9aeba2dc6f20aef85769bc83eaae8e345cb82ae6). Independent 92,603-byte nvdisasm (SHA-256 dd862d50b376cc5ae8cd083b71d5da500a751b44c4016ab5d6b3c838960cbc7e) and 349,080-byte cuobjdump (SHA-256 44f234c740a4c6f1b3dd917b979a9f495e7e838343130185febc9c604a3e84ca) views each retained exactly 80 IMMA.SP instructions: 20 m16n8k32, 40 m16n8k64, and 20 m16n8k128. The 16 raw and four compatibility calls added by this batch account for 20 instructions. The coverage kernel is unlaunched, so runtime validation remains unexecuted. Tool `/usr/local/cuda-13.3/lib64/libnvJitLink.so.13.3.33` reports `V13.3.33` (SHA-256 `3ba1e744347cd68617b862eccfd98b125482e882b7a6319f42abc9a768513db8`). Artifact `cubin`.
- `mma_sp_m16n8k64_s32_s4_satfinite`: runtime `unexecuted`
  - `llvm_nvptx` uses `inline_ptx` from profile `rust-llvm-22.1.2-1cb4e383` at PTX 7.1 / sm_80+: status `validated` (`LLVM version 22.1.2-rust-1.96.0-nightly`, SHA-256 `ff1dcdc18a5441003d43c5df267b43e2b6dd3efccca38c20bb2801885626331e`)
    - backend codegen on `sm_80, ptx71`: `succeeded` — The 7,822-byte combined public-order inline-PTX fixture (SHA-256 ea46feacc7fcd1c5ae768178ec7ad5cb97a85c892ca6317fbe4a13c72dafbc33) covered all eight m16n8k64 variants with selectors 0 and 1. Pinned LLVM emitted 5,081-byte PTX (SHA-256 d1c8848ea43659574ab6881b36791fa812f2c672b010e37cf735b83a999cd0e8) containing exactly 16 standard m16n8k64 instructions. An auxiliary 6,540-byte typed LLVM fixture (SHA-256 9e11fe0ab804292311e9d13d90a5acb1e8478b044b4110868f7ae5774e762e21) used A2, B2, C4, metadata, selector source order and emitted 22,042-byte PTX (SHA-256 837238a344ec6c789a2c63b970466e299da430cebebcd8dda7b6ab56426c633c) with the same 16 forms. The imported record has ten i32 arguments, four i32 results, ImmArg<arg9>, IntrNoCallback, IntrNoMem, and Range<arg9,0,2>. Pinned LLVM rejected selector 2 (487-byte fixture SHA-256 964fe7dd3cd81b441fd4d38cccac420cfea6d219a27208edf408171f26a97c81) and a dynamic selector (510-byte fixture SHA-256 fc193d79e8e3cfc487cd1aa3b4c312c08efc8d5c34998c3221eba7f423480908). Every standard-metadata nibble must encode two distinct 2-bit indices; 0x0, 0x5, 0xa, and 0xf are undefined behavior. This record retained exact saturating s4-by-s4 PTX.
    - PTX assembly on `sm_80, ptx71`: `succeeded` — CUDA 13.3 ptxas accepted selectors 0 and 1 at PTX 7.1/sm_80 (233-byte fixtures SHA-256 2fdee0d588d2b4f587e59da2ad4e5778a19c69e887017ac331d9cd359b19c794 and 197f1324281ada10b16a5dbd4f65c13808109180b50d29cc5f4524dd849017bb), issued only the ordered-metadata performance advisory, and assembled the full LLVM output to a 4,008-byte cubin (SHA-256 512144584ed413fe89362c698e35b2b128fd144f02b4fa70a26161ae314f203a) retaining exactly 16 IMMA.SP.16864 instructions. It rejected selector 2 (SHA-256 c2d7a5528fa41ef8d04c8e900da46bc41d5cc73e1764c0d10c60aad2e2b6730e), a register selector (SHA-256 d4f972b7c70e68d2b62c86aa181d2eecc73f3bf10e89c570bc478db51218bcb5), PTX 7.0 (SHA-256 d88934934d5bf9fbda0910bd70eadd600e98a4b31bba50e090361dd821bbc90b), and sm_75 (SHA-256 19bf3cb2fa6b19a1e7c1c4cc62c6195b3c8360ebba5785cbc89d46377650c1fa). Tool `/usr/local/cuda-13.3/bin/ptxas` reports `CUDA 13.3 V13.3.33 build cuda_13.3.r13.3/compiler.37862127_0` (SHA-256 `7fdd01a4cf50e30746da98989c9272a907f491e6fd7fecfda14642e4375f88fb`). Artifact `cubin`.

The 16 raw and four compatibility calls added by this batch account for 20 instructions. The coverage kernel is unlaunched, so runtime validation remains unexecuted. Tool `/usr/local/cuda-13.3/lib64/libnvJitLink.so.13.3.33` reports `V13.3.33` (SHA-256 `3ba1e744347cd68617b862eccfd98b125482e882b7a6319f42abc9a768513db8`). Artifact `cubin`.

    - PTX assembly on `sm_80, ptx71`: `succeeded` — CUDA 13.3 ptxas accepted selectors 0 and 1 at PTX 7.1/sm_80 (233-byte fixtures SHA-256 2fdee0d588d2b4f587e59da2ad4e5778a19c69e887017ac331d9cd359b19c794 and 197f1324281ada10b16a5dbd4f65c13808109180b50d29cc5f4524dd849017bb), issued only the ordered-metadata performance advisory, and assembled the full LLVM output to a 4,008-byte cubin (SHA-256 512144584ed413fe89362c698e35b2b128fd144f02b4fa70a26161ae314f203a) retaining exactly 16 IMMA.SP.16864 instructions. It rejected selector 2 (SHA-256 c2d7a5528fa41ef8d04c8e900da46bc41d5cc73e1764c0d10c60aad2e2b6730e), a register selector (SHA-256 d4f972b7c70e68d2b62c86aa181d2eecc73f3bf10e89c570bc478db51218bcb5), PTX 7.0 (SHA-256 d88934934d5bf9fbda0910bd70eadd600e98a4b31bba50e090361dd821bbc90b), and sm_75 (SHA-256 19bf3cb2fa6b19a1e7c1c4cc62c6195b3c8360ebba5785cbc89d46377650c1fa). Tool `/usr/local/cuda-13.3/bin/ptxas` reports `CUDA 13.3 V13.3.33 build cuda_13.3.r13.3/compiler.37862127_0` (SHA-256 `7fdd01a4cf50e30746da98989c9272a907f491e6fd7fecfda14642e4375f88fb`). Artifact `cubin`.
  - `lib_nvvm` uses `inline_ptx` from profile `cuda-13.3-libnvvm-13.3.33` at PTX 7.1 / sm_80+: status `validated` (`CUDA toolkit 13.3; nvcc/libNVVM release V13.3.33`, SHA-256 `b6a5db233133fb0ed693f8cb8aeb1d405254ce0ab10364478985292ec98cfcf0`)
    - backend codegen on `compute_80, ptx93`: `succeeded` — The same 7,822-byte NVVM IR fixture (SHA-256 ea46feacc7fcd1c5ae768178ec7ad5cb97a85c892ca6317fbe4a13c72dafbc33) passed CUDA 13.3 libNVVM and produced 4,979-byte NUL-terminated PTX (SHA-256 abe6ae9238f6137de91c9c2214fc9d7aa78da2bdd6d60a75013f45866c60c38f) containing all eight variants with selectors 0 and 1. Every standard-metadata nibble must encode two distinct 2-bit indices; 0x0, 0x5, 0xa, and 0xf are undefined behavior. This record retained exact wrapping u4-by-s4 PTX. Production cargo oxide emit-ltoir generated_intrinsics --arch=sm_80 emitted 374,720-byte NVVM IR (SHA-256 d8180177c4f4e54c641636f7d894fa01314ca6c9251a423ef9027e1985ae120b) and 104,032-byte LTOIR (SHA-256 9f451bb1d66eb39596d0639eb95cf2712e82975f518cf89829eaf18fb5fb15f0). Its 25-byte sm_80 target manifest (SHA-256 06ecfb1b0100fd9886f26871a9fc48350b612a35370b154b07d83a3a57fbbadb) and 49-byte compile-options file (SHA-256 069d630cf5359ec49a571d9074d106aeda207bc279693d2a9ce3c21b168ad03a) pin the production inputs.
    - PTX assembly on `sm_80, ptx93`: `succeeded` — After removing libNVVM's single trailing NUL, CUDA 13.3 ptxas accepted the 4,978-byte PTX (SHA-256 7b24a91e649c24cd57c20d6923a0940730b9465107178b227921b8d5f0a478d2), issued only the ordered-metadata performance advisory, and produced a 4,008-byte cubin (SHA-256 109d906a6082234019e136181d47d7306c65edbedf5c47e3c46ef56635fa6ca6) with exactly 16 IMMA.SP.16864 instructions. The PTX 7.0, sm_75, selector 2, and dynamic-selector negatives are recorded by the paired pinned-LLVM evidence. Tool `/usr/local/cuda-13.3/bin/ptxas` reports `CUDA 13.3 V13.3.33 build cuda_13.3.r13.3/compiler.37862127_0` (SHA-256 `7fdd01a4cf50e30746da98989c9272a907f491e6fd7fecfda14642e4375f88fb`). Artifact `cubin`.
    - device link on `sm_80, ptx93`: `succeeded` — The direct CUDA 13.3 libNVVM-to-nvJitLink route produced a 4,008-byte cubin with SHA-256 3cc463ee7fdde107bbd51039c7a2259f62196923d049c78703e3662cccc378f5 retaining exactly 16 IMMA.SP.16864 instructions. Production linking of that LTOIR through the repository nvJitLink helper produced a 30,952-byte sm_80 cubin (SHA-256 9c9f270e43b9732e814f899d9aeba2dc6f20aef85769bc83eaae8e345cb82ae6). Independent 92,603-byte nvdisasm (SHA-256 dd862d50b376cc5ae8cd083b71d5da500a751b44c4016ab5d6b3c838960cbc7e) and 349,080-byte cuobjdump (SHA-256 44f234c740a4c6f1b3dd917b979a9f495e7e838343130185febc9c604a3e84ca) views each retained exactly 80 IMMA.SP instructions: 20 m16n8k32, 40 m16n8k64, and 20 m16n8k128. The 16 raw and four compatibility calls added by this batch account for 20 instructions. The coverage kernel is unlaunched, so runtime validation remains unexecuted. Tool `/usr/local/cuda-13.3/lib64/libnvJitLink.so.13.3.33` reports `V13.3.33` (SHA-256 `3ba1e744347cd68617b862eccfd98b125482e882b7a6319f42abc9a768513db8`). Artifact `cubin`.

An auxiliary 4,507-byte typed LLVM comparison (SHA-256 9f0a5a680b4fe7c56ea592123f93013d81441cbcafadb159086bd9e175bcfe4a) used source A, B, C, metadata, selector order and emitted the same eight forms; this record retained exact wrapping u8-by-u8 PTX with selector 0.
    - PTX assembly on `sm_80, ptx71`: `succeeded` — CUDA 13.3 ptxas accepted all eight exact instructions at PTX 7.1 and sm_80, issued only the expected ordered-metadata performance advisory, and produced a 1,464-byte cubin with SHA-256 6bcec1b1eab13da703fdade053704aa6ab00591a1b38dd7daf48ac0dec541df2. Selector 1, PTX 7.0, and sm_75 were rejected at their exact boundaries. Because every probe function was unreferenced, ptxas removed their bodies; this proves assembly acceptance, not retained SASS.     - backend codegen on `sm_80, ptx71`: `succeeded` — The 5,051-byte combined inline-PTX probe (SHA-256 9ee9ee8e912fe4dd2b4253197b4fd9a90b70be8a506fd60e9be75e6295866dea) used public C, A, B, metadata, selector order and covered all eight variants. Pinned LLVM emitted 21,538-byte PTX (SHA-256 0ffaffff93d55a366ffac2298876adf9274cd109084fcb9194a25ad08dff074c) with exactly eight base m16n8k64 instructions at PTX 7.1 and sm_80. An auxiliary 4,507-byte typed LLVM comparison (SHA-256 9f0a5a680b4fe7c56ea592123f93013d81441cbcafadb159086bd9e175bcfe4a) used source A, B, C, metadata, selector order and emitted the same eight forms; this record retained exact wrapping u8-by-s8 PTX with selector 0.
    - PTX assembly on `sm_80, ptx71`: `succeeded` — CUDA 13.3 ptxas accepted all eight exact instructions at PTX 7.1 and sm_80, issued only the expected ordered-metadata performance advisory, and produced a 1,464-byte cubin with SHA-256 6bcec1b1eab13da703fdade053704aa6ab00591a1b38dd7daf48ac0dec541df2. Selector 1, PTX 7.0, and sm_75 were rejected at their exact boundaries. Because every probe function was unreferenced, ptxas removed their bodies; this proves assembly acceptance, not retained SASS. Tool `/usr/local/cuda-13.3/bin/ptxas` reports `CUDA 13.3 V13.3.33 build cuda_13.3.r13.3/compiler.37862127_0` (SHA-256 `7fdd01a4cf50e30746da98989c9272a907f491e6fd7fecfda14642e4375f88fb`).
  - `lib_nvvm` uses `inline_ptx` from profile `cuda-13.3-libnvvm-13.3.33` at PTX 7.1 / sm_80+: status `validated` (`CUDA toolkit 13.3; nvcc/libNVVM release V13.3.33`, SHA-256 `b6a5db233133fb0ed693f8cb8aeb1d405254ce0ab10364478985292ec98cfcf0`)
    - backend codegen on `compute_80, ptx93`: `succeeded` — The production generated_intrinsics route produced 269,916-byte compute_80 NVVM IR with SHA-256 976d4c2cb32144fc4c6c9923096685fbe1f34306bdcfd2b9b706764508183b78 and 75,912-byte LTOIR with SHA-256 bf01957e2400a1ba5a82246a85b584c1964536bf529a581b64ef4192e2b8a157. All eight raw variants and two compatibility-path calls use compile-time selector 0.
    - device link on `sm_80, ptx93`: `succeeded` — nvJitLink produced a 25,192-byte cubin with SHA-256 c1f9df611c9a113b3830ee6f837232382b95f104c4928668af27a181162afebb. Disassembly contained 40 IMMA.SP instructions: exactly 20 IMMA.SP.16864 instructions (10 new standard calls plus 10 existing ordered calls) and 20 IMMA.SP.16832 instructions. Tool `/usr/local/cuda-13.3/lib64/libnvJitLink.so.13.3.33` reports `V13.3.33` (SHA-256 `3ba1e744347cd68617b862eccfd98b125482e882b7a6319f42abc9a768513db8`). Artifact `cubin`.

The ordered half contains all eight raw variants plus two representative compatibility-path calls. Tool `/usr/local/cuda-13.3/lib64/libnvJitLink.so.13.3.33` reports `V13.3.33` (SHA-256 `3ba1e744347cd68617b862eccfd98b125482e882b7a6319f42abc9a768513db8`). Artifact `cubin`.

An auxiliary 6,948-byte typed LLVM comparison (SHA-256 379488c94c4e3d6b1806928d72723845b7bd329857f4dfc3caf8f74d7a5c6649) used source A, B, C, metadata, selector order and emitted 22,330-byte PTX (SHA-256 913b74cc0dba68779655073b83e5a534bcf076fa5e6a1d1d6029ef951b0fc1cd) with the same 16 forms; this record retained wrapping s4-by-s4 PTX.
    - PTX assembly on `sm_80, ptx85`: `succeeded` — CUDA 13.3 ptxas accepted all 16 instructions and produced a 4,008-byte cubin with SHA-256 a6df23e0492e6f26ede3da7f7fab6c9fdab2693c5c2d6bda12339948cba640ec containing exactly 16 IMMA.SP.16864 instructions. Pinned LLVM emitted 13,390-byte PTX (SHA-256 b370bcbebbe761bd65675ad2d2602230cc315189a1bb8cb1c4533c68b564a897) with exactly eight ordered m16n8k64 instructions at PTX 8.5 and sm_80; this record retained exact wrapping s8-by-s8 PTX with selector 0.
    - PTX assembly on `sm_80, ptx85`: `succeeded` — CUDA 13.3 ptxas accepted all eight exact instructions and produced a 1,464-byte cubin with SHA-256 6bcec1b1eab13da703fdade053704aa6ab00591a1b38dd7daf48ac0dec541df2. Because every probe function was unreferenced, ptxas removed their bodies; this proves assembly acceptance, not retained SASS. Tool `/usr/local/cuda-13.3/bin/ptxas` reports `CUDA 13.3 V13.3.33 build cuda_13.3.r13.3/compiler.37862127_0` (SHA-256 `7fdd01a4cf50e30746da98989c9272a907f491e6fd7fecfda14642e4375f88fb`).

All eight raw variants use compile-time selector 0.
    - device link on `sm_80, ptx93`: `succeeded` — nvJitLink produced a 23,656-byte cubin with SHA-256 8a09455f87f3ef0c7c16adf13bf6030cbe7f0f18df7fcd91ec2a19a10de5b50d. Disassembly contained 30 IMMA.SP instructions in total and exactly 10 IMMA.SP.16864 instructions: all eight raw variants plus two compatibility-path calls. Tool `/usr/local/cuda-13.3/lib64/libnvJitLink.so.13.3.33` reports `V13.3.33` (SHA-256 `3ba1e744347cd68617b862eccfd98b125482e882b7a6319f42abc9a768513db8`). Artifact `cubin`.

    - declaration canonicalization on `sm_30, ptx60`: `succeeded` — LLVM recognized the declaration and preserved its convergent, nocallback, and inaccessible-memory attributes.
    - backend codegen on `sm_30, ptx60`: `succeeded` — The selected Rust LLVM backend emitted all four reachable shfl.sync.down.b32 operand forms with the fixed clamp.
    - PTX assembly on `sm_75, ptx60`: `succeeded` — CUDA 13.3 ptxas accepted every generated register and immediate form. Tool `/usr/local/cuda-13.3/bin/ptxas` reports `CUDA 13.3 V13.3.33 build cuda_13.3.r13.3/compiler.37862127_0` (SHA-256 `7fdd01a4cf50e30746da98989c9272a907f491e6fd7fecfda14642e4375f88fb`).
  - `lib_nvvm` uses `typed_nvvm` from profile `cuda-13.3-libnvvm-13.3.33` at PTX 6.0 / sm_75+: status `validated` (`CUDA toolkit 13.3; nvcc/libNVVM release V13.3.33`, SHA-256 `b6a5db233133fb0ed693f8cb8aeb1d405254ce0ab10364478985292ec98cfcf0`)
    - declaration canonicalization on `sm_75, ptx93`: `succeeded` — The pinned LLVM 22 probe independently established the modern typed declaration's convergent, nocallback, and inaccessible-memory attributes before cuda-oxide legalized it for libNVVM.
    - backend codegen on `sm_75, ptx93`: `succeeded` — CUDA-oxide rewrote the modern typed call to libNVVM's legacy aggregate shuffle intrinsic, preserving the mode, value bits, lane or delta, and fixed clamp. CUDA 13.3 libNVVM accepted that IR and produced LTOIR.
    - device link on `sm_75, ptx93`: `succeeded` — libNVVM produced 5,944-byte LTOIR (SHA-256 bb1bd56ee46b4bce228e99e2e2cfac099d39a64c497c0a1cf87615dbf294c7be); nvJitLink produced a 5,160-byte cubin (SHA-256 ce2b2ca9ac1d39f4833c0549a743a238db490d820a6bc8f5f51a9fa89be77a38); cuobjdump showed two SHFL.IDX, SHFL.BFLY, SHFL.DOWN, and SHFL.UP instructions. Tool `/usr/local/cuda-13.3/targets/x86_64-linux/lib/libnvJitLink.so.13.3.33` reports `V13.3.33` (SHA-256 `3ba1e744347cd68617b862eccfd98b125482e882b7a6319f42abc9a768513db8`). Artifact `cubin`.

    - device link on `sm_75, ptx93`: `succeeded` — libNVVM produced 5,944-byte LTOIR (SHA-256 bb1bd56ee46b4bce228e99e2e2cfac099d39a64c497c0a1cf87615dbf294c7be); nvJitLink produced a 5,160-byte cubin (SHA-256 ce2b2ca9ac1d39f4833c0549a743a238db490d820a6bc8f5f51a9fa89be77a38); cuobjdump showed two SHFL.IDX, SHFL.BFLY, SHFL.DOWN, and SHFL.UP instructions. Tool `/usr/local/cuda-13.3/targets/x86_64-linux/lib/libnvJitLink.so.13.3.33` reports `V13.3.33` (SHA-256 `3ba1e744347cd68617b862eccfd98b125482e882b7a6319f42abc9a768513db8`). Artifact `cubin`.

CUDA 13.3 has no sm_101a assembler spelling; LLVM 22 supplies the compatibility spelling. The same exact fixture was rejected for every family on sm_120a and sm_121a, so those architectures are deliberately outside this closed target contract.     - backend codegen on `compute_100a, ptx93`: `succeeded` — CUDA 13.3 libNVVM verified the combined module and retained all 24 exact tcgen05 instructions through LTOIR and linked PTX. LTOIR SHA-256 was 234ddb423fd986e4ac7df3c50c265734fe851f8fcd3fc97827a5a70f7c1f56b3 for compute_100a, ca5840112666fac59bde59b8a7777f66bc864e5a8e2832a122b458ce38485f50 for compute_103a, and 3545a605caea9c50008d7ecb8ab1c41f7ffeea129e791baf79eef522608f33bf for compute_110a.     - PTX assembly on `sm_100a, ptx86`: `succeeded` — CUDA 13.3 ptxas accepted all 24 routes on sm_100a, sm_103a, and sm_110a. Cubin SHA-256 was e36d4a8295fb2b51a11aba88b85fd5c2cab86842963f048969b6a99c219766a6, 3a290520b622b59caae3186c109b80d1a5e79e2c24a93fc5611225dfcd39b829, and 245611da00f4fda4886614f0d49f9bf1b1a548d5002f4a31b58ec54093de65f4. CUDA 13.3 has no sm_101a assembler spelling; LLVM 22 supplies the compatibility spelling. The same exact fixture was rejected for every family on sm_120a and sm_121a, so those architectures are deliberately outside this closed target contract. Tool `/usr/local/cuda-13.3/bin/ptxas` reports `CUDA 13.3 V13.3.33 build cuda_13.3.r13.3/compiler.37862127_0` (SHA-256 `7fdd01a4cf50e30746da98989c9272a907f491e6fd7fecfda14642e4375f88fb`). Artifact `cubin`.
  - `lib_nvvm` uses `inline_ptx` from profile `cuda-13.3-libnvvm-13.3.33-tcgen05` at PTX 8.6 / sm_100a or sm_103a or sm_110a: status `validated` (`CUDA toolkit 13.3; nvcc/libNVVM release V13.3.33`, SHA-256 `b6a5db233133fb0ed693f8cb8aeb1d405254ce0ab10364478985292ec98cfcf0`)
    - backend codegen on `compute_100a, ptx93`: `succeeded` — CUDA 13.3 libNVVM verified the combined module and retained all 24 exact tcgen05 instructions through LTOIR and linked PTX. LTOIR SHA-256 was 234ddb423fd986e4ac7df3c50c265734fe851f8fcd3fc97827a5a70f7c1f56b3 for compute_100a, ca5840112666fac59bde59b8a7777f66bc864e5a8e2832a122b458ce38485f50 for compute_103a, and 3545a605caea9c50008d7ecb8ab1c41f7ffeea129e791baf79eef522608f33bf for compute_110a. Linked PTX SHA-256 was 18b127dd78c7a7ed3ca0b08a1e24d73c0ca32e2d29d51c726ddbde71f60eb7a9, 7b8944889d6d4ee5ce99639ce869f841437ef9b181f4449eb402c9004844758b, and 06431b22f61484cf3b51e7e2090acd747f4a515a219cbf53bdec284797a1729c. A direct compute_101a probe failed before code generation with NVVM_ERROR_INVALID_INPUT, so this backend route excludes sm_101a. The BF16 public route intentionally emitted PTX kind::f16 and no kind::bf16.
    - device link on `sm_100a, ptx93`: `succeeded` — CUDA 13.3 nvJitLink accepted every route on sm_100a, sm_103a, and sm_110a. Cubin SHA-256 was 1bab3647f9af53b36a52caa8323aeeacdf4244386f1ab0584e9823dcc1f13014, 57f3f660825a5d2970b34127c72cee000291ae352e4a33b508998bb0410d8a75, and 9bb5d9ded614b742960d19ed430d0f91a0fb8c90e675c7912c664cbad695a2de. - `tcgen05_ld_16x128b_x16_pack16`: runtime `not recorded`
  - `llvm_nvptx` uses `inline_ptx` from profile `rust-llvm-22.1.2-1cb4e383` at PTX 8.6 / sm_100a or sm_101a or sm_103a or sm_110a: status `validated` (`LLVM version 22.1.2-rust-1.96.0-nightly`, SHA-256 `ff1dcdc18a5441003d43c5df267b43e2b6dd3efccca38c20bb2801885626331e`)
    - backend codegen on `sm_100a, ptx86`: `succeeded` — The 29 imported LLVM records each have arguments [tmem_ptr, i1], their exact scalar or vector result, ImmArg<arg1>, IntrArgMemOnly, IntrConvergent, and NoCapture<arg0>. Each raw leaf fixes arg1 to false; each pack16 leaf fixes arg1 to true. Pinned LLVM 22 at -O0 accepted the final 58-kernel inline-PTX fixture (source SHA-256 7d3e9d0c1cd4b46a66699fe5f1a651b55a9151bdb13eb7ad5ed5c605c1e61894). Every target retained 58 entry kernels, 58 exact loads, and 29 pack::16b forms. PTX SHA-256 was 69c0fb5696fb664a099e5120f0d18ad18f00dff1ebd50100ca9645387d98b1d0 for sm_100a at PTX 8.6, b4b66448a03f9b7e3c6c18463c68cf237c68465ed69837ead3474b2bf61c05de for sm_101a at PTX 8.6, bcaa35e58cc69b6d854dbc344463470a1b7a7c0b0aead0d68db1df89ce155b5e for sm_103a at PTX 8.8, and 481483b98b8657f6731442523b07feaa9eef37a1f1b39dc1f21a0be3db3c5928 for sm_110a at PTX 9.0. The reviewed direct route is exact to those four architectures.
    - backend codegen on `sm_100a, ptx85`: `failed` — Pinned LLVM rejected sm_100a with PTX 8.5 before emission because this target requires PTX 8.6.
    - PTX assembly on `sm_100a, ptx86`: `succeeded` — CUDA 13.3 ptxas produced cubin SHA-256 ee832a6286fde07b52a7800a10911cb756a12793de68daf6fb4e6f94f7578f04 for sm_100a, 7fed294ec249f9e8278a316ba46cd9133bab10a03f1be5d57164307e710849c8 for sm_103a, and c2ca947c990b23f1287def0e60eee881ce5e4264b2c439179ce43f8ff63fc160 for sm_110a. Each cubin contains 58 entry symbols and nvdisasm found exactly 58 LDTM instructions, including 29 PACK16BIT forms. CUDA 13.3 has no sm_101a assembler spelling; its final-source PTX still contains all 58 entry kernels and exact loads. Tool `/usr/local/cuda-13.3/bin/ptxas` reports `CUDA 13.3 V13.3.33 build cuda_13.3.r13.3/compiler.37862127_0` (SHA-256 `7fdd01a4cf50e30746da98989c9272a907f491e6fd7fecfda14642e4375f88fb`). PTX SHA-256 was 501f6c2d213c73d82415acba415756ff5c2c74efd52b6a0f7685e3d1eb8ec4e2 for sm_100a, 76a6707a7d232e6d3a3f62918d29e87996bf8d1f0ca5ed35ece6a4624921a8e8 for sm_101a, bdcfe7903077855ff7f40ce0b7a9902be054ebd3bc596789b4549336ea67bbae for sm_103a, and 7b0f6907d1db4f58adbb9d94f82a2dcf0d4bc5ff8e5cec918e51b635f56c737d for sm_110a. The BF16 public route intentionally emitted PTX kind::f16 and no kind::bf16.
    - PTX assembly on `sm_100a, ptx86`: `succeeded` — CUDA 13.3 ptxas accepted all 24 routes on sm_100a, sm_103a, and sm_110a. Cubin SHA-256 was e36d4a8295fb2b51a11aba88b85fd5c2cab86842963f048969b6a99c219766a6, 3a290520b622b59caae3186c109b80d1a5e79e2c24a93fc5611225dfcd39b829, and 245611da00f4fda4886614f0d49f9bf1b1a548d5002f4a31b58ec54093de65f4. CUDA 13.3 has no sm_101a assembler spelling; LLVM 22 supplies the compatibility spelling. The same exact fixture was rejected for every family on sm_120a and sm_121a, so those architectures are deliberately outside this closed target contract. Tool `/usr/local/cuda-13.3/bin/ptxas` reports `CUDA 13.3 V13.3.33 build cuda_13.3.r13.3/compiler.37862127_0` (SHA-256 `7fdd01a4cf50e30746da98989c9272a907f491e6fd7fecfda14642e4375f88fb`). Artifact `cubin`.
  - `lib_nvvm` uses `inline_ptx` from profile `cuda-13.3-libnvvm-13.3.33-tcgen05` at PTX 8.6 / sm_100a or sm_103a or sm_110a: status `validated` (`CUDA toolkit 13.3; nvcc/libNVVM release V13.3.33`, SHA-256 `b6a5db233133fb0ed693f8cb8aeb1d405254ce0ab10364478985292ec98cfcf0`)
    - backend codegen on `compute_100a, ptx93`: `succeeded` — CUDA 13.3 libNVVM verified the combined module and retained all 24 exact tcgen05 instructions through LTOIR and linked PTX. LTOIR SHA-256 was 234ddb423fd986e4ac7df3c50c265734fe851f8fcd3fc97827a5a70f7c1f56b3 for compute_100a, ca5840112666fac59bde59b8a7777f66bc864e5a8e2832a122b458ce38485f50 for compute_103a, and 3545a605caea9c50008d7ecb8ab1c41f7ffeea129e791baf79eef522608f33bf for compute_110a. Linked PTX SHA-256 was 18b127dd78c7a7ed3ca0b08a1e24d73c0ca32e2d29d51c726ddbde71f60eb7a9, 7b8944889d6d4ee5ce99639ce869f841437ef9b181f4449eb402c9004844758b, and 06431b22f61484cf3b51e7e2090acd747f4a515a219cbf53bdec284797a1729c. A direct compute_101a probe failed before code generation with NVVM_ERROR_INVALID_INPUT, so this backend route excludes sm_101a. The BF16 public route intentionally emitted PTX kind::f16 and no kind::bf16.
    - device link on `sm_100a, ptx93`: `succeeded` — CUDA 13.3 nvJitLink accepted every route on sm_100a, sm_103a, and sm_110a. Cubin SHA-256 was 1bab3647f9af53b36a52caa8323aeeacdf4244386f1ab0584e9823dcc1f13014, 57f3f660825a5d2970b34127c72cee000291ae352e4a33b508998bb0410d8a75, and 9bb5d9ded614b742960d19ed430d0f91a0fb8c90e675c7912c664cbad695a2de. The same exact LTO route reached PTX but cubin linking rejected every family on sm_120a and sm_121a; those architectures are deliberately excluded. Tool `/usr/local/cuda-13.3/lib64/libnvJitLink.so.13.3.33` reports `V13.3.33` (SHA-256 `3ba1e744347cd68617b862eccfd98b125482e882b7a6319f42abc9a768513db8`). Artifact `cubin`.
- `tcgen05_ld_16x256b_x8_raw`: runtime `not recorded`
  - `llvm_nvptx` uses `inline_ptx` from profile `rust-llvm-22.1.2-1cb4e383` at PTX 8.6 / sm_100a or sm_101a or sm_103a or sm_110a: status `validated` (`LLVM version 22.1.2-rust-1.96.0-nightly`, SHA-256 `ff1dcdc18a5441003d43c5df267b43e2b6dd3efccca38c20bb2801885626331e`)
    - backend codegen on `sm_100a, ptx86`: `succeeded` — The 29 imported LLVM records each have arguments [tmem_ptr, i1], their exact scalar or vector result, ImmArg<arg1>, IntrArgMemOnly, IntrConvergent, and NoCapture<arg0>. Each raw leaf fixes arg1 to false; each pack16 leaf fixes arg1 to true. Pinned LLVM 22 at -O0 accepted the final 58-kernel inline-PTX fixture (source SHA-256 7d3e9d0c1cd4b46a66699fe5f1a651b55a9151bdb13eb7ad5ed5c605c1e61894). Every target retained 58 entry kernels, 58 exact loads, and 29 pack::16b forms. PTX SHA-256 was 69c0fb5696fb664a099e5120f0d18ad18f00dff1ebd50100ca9645387d98b1d0 for sm_100a at PTX 8.6, b4b66448a03f9b7e3c6c18463c68cf237c68465ed69837ead3474b2bf61c05de for sm_101a at PTX 8.6, bcaa35e58cc69b6d854dbc344463470a1b7a7c0b0aead0d68db1df89ce155b5e for sm_103a at PTX 8.8, and 481483b98b8657f6731442523b07feaa9eef37a1f1b39dc1f21a0be3db3c5928 for sm_110a at PTX 9.0. The reviewed direct route is exact to those four architectures.
    - backend codegen on `sm_100a, ptx85`: `failed` — Pinned LLVM rejected sm_100a with PTX 8.5 before emission because this target requires PTX 8.6.
    - PTX assembly on `sm_100a, ptx86`: `succeeded` — CUDA 13.3 ptxas produced cubin SHA-256 ee832a6286fde07b52a7800a10911cb756a12793de68daf6fb4e6f94f7578f04 for sm_100a, 7fed294ec249f9e8278a316ba46cd9133bab10a03f1be5d57164307e710849c8 for sm_103a, and c2ca947c990b23f1287def0e60eee881ce5e4264b2c439179ce43f8ff63fc160 for sm_110a. Each cubin contains 58 entry symbols and nvdisasm found exactly 58 LDTM instructions, including 29 PACK16BIT forms. CUDA 13.3 has no sm_101a assembler spelling; its final-source PTX still contains all 58 entry kernels and exact loads. Tool `/usr/local/cuda-13.3/bin/ptxas` reports `CUDA 13.3 V13.3.33 build cuda_13.3.r13.3/compiler.37862127_0` (SHA-256 `7fdd01a4cf50e30746da98989c9272a907f491e6fd7fecfda14642e4375f88fb`). Artifact `cubin`.

Tool `/usr/local/cuda-13.3/bin/ptxas` reports `CUDA 13.3 V13.3.33 build cuda_13.3.r13.3/compiler.37862127_0` (SHA-256 `7fdd01a4cf50e30746da98989c9272a907f491e6fd7fecfda14642e4375f88fb`). Artifact `cubin`.
  - `lib_nvvm` uses `inline_ptx` from profile `cuda-13.3-libnvvm-13.3.33-tcgen05` at PTX 8.6 / sm_100a or sm_103a or sm_110a: status `validated` (`CUDA toolkit 13.3; nvcc/libNVVM release V13.3.33`, SHA-256 `b6a5db233133fb0ed693f8cb8aeb1d405254ce0ab10364478985292ec98cfcf0`)
    - backend codegen on `compute_100a, ptx93`: `succeeded` — CUDA 13.3 libNVVM verified the combined module and retained all 24 exact tcgen05 instructions through LTOIR and linked PTX. LTOIR SHA-256 was 234ddb423fd986e4ac7df3c50c265734fe851f8fcd3fc97827a5a70f7c1f56b3 for compute_100a, ca5840112666fac59bde59b8a7777f66bc864e5a8e2832a122b458ce38485f50 for compute_103a, and 3545a605caea9c50008d7ecb8ab1c41f7ffeea129e791baf79eef522608f33bf for compute_110a. Linked PTX SHA-256 was 18b127dd78c7a7ed3ca0b08a1e24d73c0ca32e2d29d51c726ddbde71f60eb7a9, 7b8944889d6d4ee5ce99639ce869f841437ef9b181f4449eb402c9004844758b, and 06431b22f61484cf3b51e7e2090acd747f4a515a219cbf53bdec284797a1729c. A direct compute_101a probe failed before code generation with NVVM_ERROR_INVALID_INPUT, so this backend route excludes sm_101a. The BF16 public route intentionally emitted PTX kind::f16 and no kind::bf16.
    - device link on `sm_100a, ptx93`: `succeeded` — CUDA 13.3 nvJitLink accepted every route on sm_100a, sm_103a, and sm_110a. Cubin SHA-256 was 1bab3647f9af53b36a52caa8323aeeacdf4244386f1ab0584e9823dcc1f13014, 57f3f660825a5d2970b34127c72cee000291ae352e4a33b508998bb0410d8a75, and 9bb5d9ded614b742960d19ed430d0f91a0fb8c90e675c7912c664cbad695a2de. The same exact LTO route reached PTX but cubin linking rejected every family on sm_120a and sm_121a; those architectures are deliberately excluded. Tool `/usr/local/cuda-13.3/lib64/libnvJitLink.so.13.3.33` reports `V13.3.33` (SHA-256 `3ba1e744347cd68617b862eccfd98b125482e882b7a6319f42abc9a768513db8`). Artifact `cubin`.
